The Kurukshetra Court Clerk merit list 2026 (result PDF) contains details of qualified candidates for the next stage (Computer Proficiency Test on 10 May 2026). Separate lists may be prepared category-wise.

Merit List / Result PDF Contains:

  • Roll Number of candidates

  • Category
  • Qualifying Status
  • Marks (if mentioned)

Types of Lists Released:

  2. Category-Wise List: For General, EWS, BCA, BCB, SC, etc.

  • ✓ Keep all original documents ready for verification

The SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 has been released by the Staff Selection Commission (SSC).

As per the latest updates, the final marks and scorecard were made available on February 17, 2026, at the official website ssc.gov.in. This follows the declaration of the SSC GD final result on January 15, 2026.

Candidates who appeared in the PST/PET and DV/DME/RME stages can now download their SSC GD final scorecard 2025 using their registration number and password. The scorecard displays normalized CBT marks and overall qualifying status after all recruitment stages. The download window is limited - typically a short period - so act quickly before the link deactivates.

Key Highlights:

  • Release Date: February 17, 2026
  • Last Date to Download: March 1, 2026 (till 6:00 PM)
  • Access: Online only via ssc.gov.in login
  • Purpose: View final marks, compare with cut-offs, and save for records/joining

SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 Overview

Particular Details
Exam Name Constable (GD) in CAPFs, SSF, NIA, Rifleman (GD) in Assam Rifles, and Sepoy in NCB Examination 2025
Conducting Body Staff Selection Commission (SSC)

Scorecard Status Released on February 17, 2026

PET/PST and Medical Stages Conducted post-CBT shortlisting
Final Result Declaration January 15, 2026
Final Scorecard Release February 17, 2026
Last Date to Download Scorecard March 1, 2026 (6:00 PM)

Direct Link to Check SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

Candidates can access the SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 through the direct link on the SSC portal under the “Result/Marks” section after logging in. The portal may redirect from legacy links, but use ssc.gov.in primarily.

SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 – Direct Link (Active)
(Login required at ssc.gov.in for marks download)

Step-by-Step Guide to Download SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

  1. Visit the official Staff Selection Commission website: ssc.gov.in.
  2. Click on the “Login” or “Candidate Login” button on the homepage.
  3. Enter your registration number/username, password, and captcha (if prompted).
  4. After logging in, navigate to the “Result/Marks” tab on your dashboard.
  5. Select “Constable (GD) in CAPFs, SSF, NIA and Rifleman (GD) in Assam Rifles Examination 2025” from the list.
  6. Click on the “SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025” or “Marks” link to view your details.
  7. Your final scorecard will display on screen - review marks, cut-off comparison, and status.
  8. Download the PDF and print it for future reference (e.g., document verification). Save it promptly, as access ends after the deadline.
  9. (Use the direct link mentioned above to reach the login faster if available.)

Details Mentioned on SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

Your scorecard typically includes:

  • Candidate’s name, roll number, registration number
  • Category/sub-category (UR, OBC, SC, ST, EWS, etc.)
  • Exam name and applied post
  • Section-wise marks (General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Knowledge & General Awareness, Elementary Mathematics, English/Hindi)
  • Raw and normalized marks
  • Total marks in CBT (considered for final merit after qualifying PET/PST and medical)
  • Qualifying status (selected/not selected) and any remarks
  • Note on limited availability window

SSC GD Final Cut Off and Merit List 2025

The SSC GD final cut off 2025 was released with the final result on January 15, 2026, and is force-wise, state-wise, category-wise, and gender-wise. It appears in the official result PDF write-up.

Your scorecard marks should be compared to the relevant cut-off for your category/force/state to assess selection. The merit list PDF lists roll numbers of provisionally selected candidates (around 50,047 selected against vacancies), while the scorecard provides individual marks for those who reached physical/medical stages.

(Refer to the official SSC result PDFs for exact cut-off tables - examples range from ~104 to 152+ depending on category/force.)

What to Do After Downloading SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

Selected candidates: Keep multiple printouts/PDFs of the scorecard and final result for document verification, joining, and force allocation.

Verify details (name, roll number, category, etc.) - report serious discrepancies to SSC regional offices promptly.

Non-selected candidates: Use marks for performance analysis and preparation for future SSC exams.

Download and save copies early - the window closes after the last date.

SSC GD Selection Process 2025 (Short Overview)

The process included:

  • Computer-Based Test (CBT): Four sections totaling 160 marks.
  • Physical Efficiency Test (PET) and Physical Standard Test (PST): Qualifying.
  • Detailed Medical Examination (DME)/Review Medical Examination (RME): Qualifying.
  • Final merit based on normalized CBT marks (for qualified candidates), followed by force/state allocation per vacancies and preferences.

The final scorecard reflects CBT performance post-qualifying stages, available briefly post-result.

  • SSC GD Final Result 2025 Merit List PDF: Available on ssc.gov.in under Results
  • SSC GD Final Cut Off 2025 PDF: In final result write-up on ssc.gov.in
  • SSC Official Website: ssc.gov.in

Details on JPSC JET Admit Card 2026

Your JPSC JET Admit Card 2026 will contain the following information:

Candidate Information:

  • Candidate Name
  • Father's Name
  • Date of Birth
  • Category (General/OBC/SC/ST/EWS)
  • Gender
  • Application/Roll Number
  • Candidate's Photograph and Signature

Exam Information:

  • Exam Name: Jharkhand Eligibility Test (JET) 2025-26
  • Exam Date: 26 April 2026
  • Exam Shift/Timing
  • Reporting Time
  • Exam Center Name and Complete Address
  • Exam Center Code

Important Instructions:

  • Documents to carry on exam day
  • Items allowed and not allowed in examination hall
  • OMR filling guidelines
  • Exam day instructions and rules

Documents Required on JPSC JET Exam Day

Mandatory Documents (Originals):

  • JPSC JET Admit Card 2026 (printed copy with clear photograph)
  • Valid Photo ID Proof (any one of the following):
    • Aadhaar Card
    • Voter ID Card
    • Driving License
    • PAN Card
    • Passport
  • Recent passport-size photographs (2 copies - same as uploaded in application)
  • Simple pen (blue/black ballpoint)

Optional but Recommended:

  • Pencil and eraser (for rough work)
  • Water bottle (transparent, without label)
  • Hand sanitizer (small bottle)

Not Allowed in Examination Hall:

  • Mobile phones and smartphones
  • Smartwatches and digital watches
  • Calculators and electronic devices
  • Books, notes, and study materials
  • Bags and pouches
  • Bluetooth devices and earphones

Important Instructions for JPSC JET Exam 2026

Before Exam Day:

  • Download admit card 2-3 days before exam date
  • Check exam center location on Google Maps
  • Plan your route and transportation
  • Arrange all required documents in advance
  • Keep backup copies of admit card
  • Read exam instructions carefully

On Exam Day:

  • Reach exam center 45-60 minutes before reporting time
  • Entry closes 30 minutes before exam starts (No late entry allowed)
  • Follow dress code if specified in admit card
  • Carry water and light snacks for outside exam hall
  • Keep calm and focused

During Exam:

  • Follow all invigilator instructions carefully
  • Fill OMR sheet with black/blue ballpoint pen only (no whitener)
  • Manage your time wisely for all sections (Paper 1 + Paper 2 - 3 Hours total)
  • Don't panic if questions seem difficult
  • Review answers if time permits
  • Don't carry prohibited items inside exam hall

What If JPSC JET Admit Card Not Available?

If you're unable to download your JPSC JET Admit Card 2026, try these solutions:

  1. Solution 1: Clear browser cache and cookies, then try again with a fresh session
  2. Solution 2: Try different web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Microsoft Edge, Safari)
  3. Solution 3: Check your application status on the official website to verify if your application was accepted
  4. Solution 4: Contact JPSC helpdesk immediately

Important: Act immediately if facing issues downloading admit card. Don't wait until exam day. Contact JPSC helpdesk at least 2 days before the exam.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has released the Selection Post Phase 14 Recruitment 2026 notification on 13 April 2026 for 2919 (Revised) vacancies across various Ministries, Departments and Offices of the Government of India and their Attached/Subordinate offices. Candidates with Matriculation (10th), Higher Secondary (10+2) and Graduation & above qualifications can apply online for various Selection Posts through the SSC website ssc.gov.in from 13 April 2026 to 04 May 2026.

SSC has updated the Selection Post Phase 14 vacancies by removing GSI posts and adding DRDO vacancies. Candidates can check the complete notification along with detailed post-wise information and codes. These posts are placed in Pay Levels 1 to 8 of the 7th CPC Pay Matrix (Group B & C), making SSC Selection Post Phase 14 a good opportunity for 10th/12th/Graduate candidates seeking Central Government jobs. The online application process has started on 13-04-2026 and will remain active till 04-05-2026, and candidates can download the official notification PDF and apply online through the direct links given below.

SSC Recruitment 2026 Selection Post Phase 14 Notification PDF

The SSC Selection Post Phase 14 Notification PDF 2026 for Matriculation, Higher Secondary and Graduate level posts with Advertisement No. Phase‑XIV/2026/Selection Posts was released by the Staff Selection Commission on 13 April 2026 at ssc.gov.in. The detailed notification provides post category-wise vacancies, region-wise vacancy distribution, eligibility, age limits, application fee, exam pattern and selection process for 2919 Selection Posts in Central, Eastern, Northern, Western and other SSC regions. Candidates should read the official notice and subsequent addendum carefully before filling the SSC Phase 14 online form.

  • Download SSC Selection Post Phase 14 Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF
  • Download Addendum to SSC Phase‑XIV/2026 Selection Posts Notice (20.04.2026)
  • Cancellation of the Posts in Geological Survey of India (GSI), M/o Mines advertised under Phase-XIV/2026/Selection Posts
  • SSC Selection Post Phase 14 Syllabus 2026 and Exam Pattern

SSC Recruitment 2026 Age Limit

  • Age limits for SSC Selection Post Phase 14 vary post-wise and fall into ranges such as 18–25, 18–27, 18–28, 18–30, 18–35, 20–25, 20–30, 21–27, 21–28, 22–28 and 23–28 years as per the User Department requirements.
Category Age Relaxation
SC / ST 5 years relaxation on upper age limit
OBC (NCL) 3 years relaxation on upper age limit
PwBD (Unreserved) 10 years
PwBD (OBC) 13 years
PwBD (SC/ST) 15 years
Ex‑Servicemen Relaxation as per Government rules (subject to maximum age limit) after deduction of military service from actual age

The exact crucial date for determining age, category-wise relaxations and upper age limits for each post are specified in the SSC Phase‑XIV/2026 notification, and candidates must refer to the post details before applying.

SSC Selection Post Phase 14 Application Fee 2026

  • For General / OBC / EWS candidates: ₹100/- examination fee to be paid online.
  • For Women, SC, ST, PwBD and Ex‑Servicemen candidates eligible for reservation: No fee (Exempted) as per SSC rules.
  • Payment Mode: Online through BHIM UPI, Net Banking, or by using Visa, MasterCard, Maestro, RuPay Credit or Debit Cards within the prescribed time limit.

SSC Selection Post Phase 14 Selection Process 2026

  • Computer Based Examination (CBE): SSC will conduct separate CBTs for Matriculation, 10+2 and Graduation & above level posts consisting of Objective Type Multiple Choice Questions with 100 questions (25 each from General Intelligence, General Awareness, Quantitative Aptitude and English Language).
  • Exam Duration: 60 minutes for general candidates and 80 minutes for eligible candidates using scribes; there is negative marking of 0.50 marks for each wrong answer.
  • Skill Tests: Skill Tests like Typing/Data Entry/Computer Proficiency Tests etc., where prescribed for particular posts, will be of qualifying nature and conducted by the User Departments.
  • Document Verification (DV): Shortlisted candidates will be called by the respective User Departments for Document Verification and final selection as per post-wise and category-wise vacancies.

SSC Selection Post Phase 14 Salary 2026

Pay Component Details
Pay Level Selection Posts are placed in Pay Levels 1 to 8 of the 7th CPC Pay Matrix, varying according to the post and User Department.
Approximate Pay Basic pay as per level plus Dearness Allowance, House Rent Allowance, Transport Allowance and other admissible allowances as per Central Government norms.

SSC Selection Post Phase 14 Documents Required

No supporting documents/certificates are required to be uploaded at the time of online application for SSC Selection Post Phase 14; however, candidates must possess the original documents for verification.

  • Matriculation, 10+2 and Graduation mark sheets and certificates to support educational qualifications.
  • Caste certificate for SC/ST/OBC, EWS certificate, PwBD certificate and Ex‑Servicemen documents where applicable, in prescribed formats.
  • Photo identity proof such as Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Driving Licence, Passport etc. for identity verification at exam and DV stages.

How to Apply for SSC Recruitment 2026 (Selection Post Phase 14)

  • Candidates must apply online only through the SSC website https://ssc.gov.in or the mySSC mobile app within the application window from 13-04-2026 to 04-05-2026 (up to 23:00 Hrs).
  • Register on the SSC portal (One‑time registration) if not already registered, using a valid mobile number and email ID, then log in to apply for Selection Post Phase 14.
  • Fill in all details in the online form such as personal information, category, educational qualification, post preferences and exam centre choices accurately as per original documents.
  • Upload scanned photograph and signature in the prescribed format and size as specified in the notification.
  • Pay the application fee (if applicable) through the available online payment modes before 05-05-2026 (up to 23:00 Hrs).
  • Use the “Application Form Correction Window” between 11-05-2026 and 13-05-2026 to correct mistakes if required and pay the correction charges as applicable.
  • After successful submission and fee payment, download/print the final submitted application form for future reference; candidates will be provisionally admitted to the examination subject to verification at later stages.

Important Instructions for Candidates

  • Before filling the online application, candidates must read the full SSC Phase‑XIV/2026 Selection Posts notice carefully and ensure that they meet all eligibility conditions for the posts they apply for.
  • All particulars filled in the online application must match the original certificates, and any mismatch detected at Document Verification will result in cancellation of candidature.
  • Application fee is non‑refundable and no request for change in category, post, exam centre or any other details will be entertained after final submission.
  • Provisional admission to the exam does not imply final selection; final appointment is subject to verification of eligibility, documents and other conditions by User Departments.
  • Candidates are advised to visit SSC’s official website and the websites of User Departments regularly for updates on exam schedule, admit cards, results and DV schedule; no separate correspondence will generally be made.
  • PwBD, Ex‑Servicemen and other reserved category candidates must possess and produce valid certificates in the prescribed format at the time of Document Verification to avail reservation/relaxation benefits.
  • Any form of canvassing, use of unfair means or providing false information will lead to disqualification and possible legal action; SSC’s decision in all matters relating to recruitment will be final and binding.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited (BPCL) has published the Entry Level Profiles Notification 2026 on 18 April 2026. The recruitment drive aims to fill 250 Entry Level Profiles positions. Candidates within the age range of up to 32 years / 35 years (post-wise) and holding Diploma, B.Com / BBA / B.Sc / BMS / BHM, M.Sc, Master’s in Hindi, or Inter CA / Inter CMA + Graduation with relevant work experience are eligible to apply online at bharatpetroleum.in. The application process will be active from 18 April 2026 to 17 May 2026, and candidates can easily access the notification PDF and apply link below.

BPCL Entry Level Profiles Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited (BPCL) has released the advertisement for recruitment to various Entry Level Profiles (Junior Executive, Associate Executive & Secretary) on regular basis. Candidates can download the official notification from the BPCL website and check profile-wise vacancies, eligibility, fee and application process before applying online.

  • Download BPCL Entry Level Profiles Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

BPCL Entry Level Profiles Recruitment 2026 Overview

Company Name Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited (BPCL)
Reference No. Recruitment - Entry Level Profiles
Post Name Junior Executive (Engineering / Accounts / Wayside Amenities), Secretary BPCL, Associate Executive (Quality Assurance / Official Language Implementation)
Total Vacancies 250 Posts 
Qualification Diploma, B. Com / BBA / B.Sc / BMS / BHM, M. Sc, Masters in Hindi, Inter CA / Inter CMA + Graduation
Application Mode Online only.
Start Date for Apply Online  18 April 2026
Last Date for Apply Online  17 May 2026 (23:59 hrs)
Official Website https://www.bharatpetroleum.in

BPCL Entry Level Profiles Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Commencement of Online Application 18 April 2026
Last Date of Online Application 17 May 2026 (23:59 hrs)

BPCL Entry Level Profiles Vacancy 2026

Indicative vacancies have been announced profile-wise. The number of vacancies is indicative and may vary based on actual requirements at the sole discretion of the Corporation.

Profile #Indicative Vacancies
Junior Executive (Engineering) 175
Junior Executive (Accounts) 25
Junior Executive (Wayside Amenities) 10
Secretary BPCL 08
Associate Executive (Quality Assurance) 30
Associate Executive (Official Language Implementation) 02
Total  250 

BPCL Entry Level Profiles Eligibility Criteria 2026

Candidates must fulfil the essential educational qualification and minimum work-experience as on 01.04.2026.

Post Name Educational Qualification Work Experience
Junior Executive (Engineering) Diploma in Engineering (3-year course) in Mechanical / Electrical / Instrumentation / Electronics / Civil / Chemical Engineering from a recognized University with minimum 55% aggregate (relaxed to 50% for SC/ST/PwBD). Minimum 5 years of continuous post-qualification relevant work experience (including minimum 1 year in managerial/supervisory role) in sectors like POL Terminal/Depot, LPG Bottling Plant, Pipeline, Refinery, Petrochemical, etc.
Junior Executive (Accounts) Graduate in any discipline with minimum 55% + Inter CA / Inter CMA. Minimum 5 years of post-qualification relevant experience in finance function.
Junior Executive (Wayside Amenities) B.Com / BBA / B.Sc / BMS / BHM with minimum 55% (relaxed to 50% for SC/ST/PwBD). Minimum 5 years of relevant post-qualification experience (at least 3 years in Facility Management / QSR / Retail / Hospitality, etc.).
Secretary Class X + Class XII + Bachelor's Degree (3-year course) with minimum 55% in each (relaxed to 50% for SC/ST/PwBD). Minimum 5 years as Secretarial Assistant / Secretary or similar roles.
Associate Executive (Quality Assurance) M.Sc (Chemistry) with specialization in specified branches with minimum 60% (relaxed to 55% for SC/ST/PwBD). Minimum 3 years post-qualification relevant work experience in laboratory in Petroleum / Oil & Gas / Petro-Chemical / Chemical industry.
Associate Executive (Official Language Implementation) Master’s in Hindi (2-year course) with minimum 55% (relaxed to 50% for SC/ST/PwBD) + English studied in Class XII / Graduation with minimum 55%. Minimum 3 years of post-qualification work experience in translation (English ↔ Hindi).

Common Note for All Profiles

  • Only Indian Nationals are eligible.

BPCL Entry Level Profiles Application Fee 2026

Age relaxations as per Presidential Directives for OBC-NCL / SC / ST / PwBD / Ex-Servicemen (cumulative maximum 10 years).

Profile Upper Age Limit (as on 01.04.2026)

Junior Executive (Wayside Amenities) 32 years
Secretary BPCL 32 years
Associate Executive (Quality Assurance) 35 years
Associate Executive (Official Language Implementation) 35 years

BPCL Entry Level Profiles Application Fee 2026

Category Application Fee
UR / OBC-NCL / EWS ₹1180/- (₹1000 + 18% GST + payment gateway charges if applicable)
SC / ST / PwBD Exempted (No Application Fee)

BPCL Entry Level Profiles Selection Process 2026

  • Multi-stage selection process may comprise Application Screening, Written / Computer Based Test, Case Based Discussion, Group Task, Personal Interview etc.
  • Provisional selection based on Relevant work-experience, Significant on-the-job achievements, Performance in various selection processes.
  • Final appointment subject to successful completion of Pre-Employment Medical Examination.

How to Apply for BPCL Recruitment 2026

Candidates must apply ONLINE only through the official BPCL website. No other mode of application will be accepted. The online application process is open from 18 April 2026 to 17 May 2026 (23:59 hrs).

Step-by-Step Application Process

  1. Check Eligibility: Ensure that you meet all the eligibility criteria including educational qualification, age limit, and work experience before applying. No eligibility-related queries will be entertained.
  2. Keep Documents Ready: Prepare scanned copies (PDF format) of the following documents:
    • Date of Birth Proof (Class 10th/12th Certificate)
    • Educational Qualification Certificates (Marksheet + Degree/Diploma)
    • Work Experience Certificates (with job role, duration, salary, etc.)
    • Caste/Category Certificate (if applicable)
    • Recent Passport Size Photograph (latest, not older than April 2026)
    • Scanned Signature (not in CAPITAL letters)
    • Last 3 Salary Slips (combined in one PDF)
  3. Register Online:
    • Visit the BPCL Careers page and click on “Apply Online”
    • Click on “New Registration”
    • Enter Name, Email ID, and Mobile Number
    • Note the Registration Number and Password generated
  4. Fill Application Form:
    • Login using credentials
    • Fill personal, educational, and experience details carefully
    • Use “Save & Next” to verify details
  5. Upload Documents: Upload photograph, signature, and required documents as per prescribed format.
  6. Preview Application: Carefully review all details using the Preview Tab before final submission. No changes will be allowed after submission.
  7. Pay Application Fee:
    • Pay via Debit Card / Credit Card / UPI / Net Banking
    • Ensure payment status shows “Completed”
    • Take a printout of payment receipt
  8. Final Submission: Click on “Submit” to complete the application process.

Important Instructions

  • Apply only once; multiple applications may lead to rejection.
  • No hard copy of application needs to be sent to BPCL.
  • Ensure all details match original documents; mismatch may lead to disqualification.
  • Keep your registered email ID and mobile number active for future communication.

BPCL Entry Level Profiles Online Application Form

Eligible candidates who wish to apply for the BPCL Entry Level Profiles can do so by submitting their application form online through the official BPCL website. The application process for the BPCL Recruitment 2026 is completely online, and candidates must ensure that they complete all steps before the deadline.

The online application process for the BPCL Entry Level Profiles Recruitment 2026 is available through the BPCL portal. Eligible candidates are encouraged to fill out the application form and submit the required documents before the last date.

Click Here to Apply Online for BPCL Entry Level Profiles Recruitment 2026

BPCL Entry Level Profiles Remuneration 2026

Designation Pay Scale* Approx. CTC at the minimum of Pay Scale
Junior Executive & Secretary Post 30,000-1,20,000 ₹12.28 Lakhs per annum
Associate Executive 40,000-1,40,000 ₹17.11 Lakhs per annum

Candidates will be inducted at the minimum of the pay scale. In addition to Basic Pay, Dearness Allowance (IDA pattern), HRA (if applicable), Perks & Allowances, Retirement Benefits and Performance Related Pay will be payable as per Corporation’s policy.

Important Instructions for Candidates

  • Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply.
  • The cutoff date for age, qualification, and experience is 01 April 2026.
  • All qualifications must be from AICTE/UGC recognized institutions.
  • Marks should be calculated on total aggregate; no rounding off allowed.
  • CGPA must be converted into percentage as per university norms.
  • Only post-qualification experience will be considered.
  • Internship/training during studies will not be counted.
  • Candidates must fill the application form carefully; details once submitted are final.
  • No changes will be allowed after final submission.
  • Submit only one application; latest application will be considered if multiple are submitted.
  • Candidates working in Govt/PSU must produce NOC during selection.
  • Shortlisting is provisional and subject to document verification.
  • Ineligible candidates at any stage will be disqualified.
  • All communication will be done via registered email only.
  • Selection process participation does not guarantee final selection.
  • Updates/corrigendum will be available only on the official website.
  • Travel Allowance (TA) will be provided as per rules for eligible candidates.
  • BPCL reserves the right to cancel/modify recruitment or change vacancies.
  • Providing false information will lead to disqualification.
  • Canvassing or external influence will result in rejection.
  • Apply only through the official portal; beware of fraudulent agents.
  • Any dispute will be subject to Mumbai jurisdiction.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The Sashastra Seema Bal (SSB) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Constable (Tradesman & Driver) posts! Published on 21–27 March 2026 at the official website ssb.gov.in, this new recruitment drive offers 827 vacancies for eligible candidates. Applicants within the 18 to 27 years age group (post-wise variation applicable) and possessing a 10th pass / Matriculation with relevant trade qualification or driving licence (for Driver post) can apply online from 21 March 2026 to 04 May 2026.

SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Notification 2026 PDF

Sashastra Seema Bal (SSB), under the Ministry of Home Affairs, Government of India, has published the detailed advertisement for Constable (Veterinary, Driver, Tradesman & Nursing Orderly) posts in the Employment News (21–27 March 2026) and on its recruitment portal. The detailed notification has been issued under Advertisement No. 524/RC/SSB/Combined Advt./CT to SI (Non-GD)/2025 by the Office of the Director General, Sashastra Seema Bal, New Delhi, and contains post-wise vacancies, eligibility, physical standards, selection process, exam pattern and application instructions. Candidates must read the official notification carefully before submitting their online application form for SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Recruitment 2026.

  • Download SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Employment News Notification PDF
  • Download SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Official Notification PDF
  • Click Here to Apply Online for SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Recruitment 2026

Similar SSB Recruitment Notifications 2026

SSB Head Constable Recruitment 2026 – 233 Posts
SSB Sub Inspector Recruitment 2026 – 51 Posts

SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Recruitment 2026 Overview

Particulars Details
Organisation Sashastra Seema Bal (SSB), Ministry of Home Affairs, Government of India
Recruitment Name Constable (Tradesman & Driver) Recruitment 2026
Advertisement No. 524/RC/SSB/Combined Advt./CT to SI (Non-GD)/2025
Posts Constable (Veterinary, Driver, Gardener, Water Carrier, Cobbler, Tailor, Washerman, Barber, Waiter, Carpenter & Nursing Orderly)
Total Vacancies 827 posts
Pay Scale Level-3 (₹21,700–69,100) of 7th CPC pay matrix
Job Category Group ‘C’ Non-Gazetted (Combatised), Non-Ministerial
Job Location Anywhere in India / outside India as per SSB requirement
Age Limit 18–27 years (post-wise age varies as per notification; crucial date is last date of application)
Application Mode Online only through SSB recruitment portal
Selection Process PET, PST, Written Exam, Trade/Skill Test, Documentation & Medical Examination
Official Website www.ssb.gov.in

SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Notification Date (Employment News) 21–27 March 2026
Online Application Start Date 21 March 2026
Last Date for Online Application Extended 20 April 2026 04-05-2026

SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Vacancy 2026

A total of 827 Constable vacancies have been announced for Veterinary, Driver and various Tradesman posts through SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Recruitment 2026. Vacancies are notified category-wise for UR, EWS, OBC, SC and ST candidates, and 10% of the total vacancies are reserved for Ex-Servicemen which will be filled by non-Ex-Servicemen if suitable Ex-Servicemen are not available.

Post Name Total Posts
Constable (Veterinary) 34
Constable (Driver) – Male only 553
Constable (Gardener) 41
Constable (Water Carrier) 5
Constable (Cobbler) 25
Constable (Tailor) 41
Constable (Washerman) 74
Constable (Barber) 43
Constable (Waiter) 3
Constable (Carpenter) 7
Constable (Nursing Orderly) 1
Grand Total 827

The Director General, Sashastra Seema Bal reserves the right to increase or decrease the number of vacancies, not to fill any vacancy and cancel the recruitment process without assigning any reason.

SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Eligibility Criteria 2026

  • Nationality: For Constables (Washerman, Barber, Water Carrier, Waiter, Tailor, Gardener, Cobbler), the candidate must be a citizen of India. For Constables (Driver, Veterinary, Carpenter, Nursing Orderly), only Indian citizens will normally be appointed, but subjects of Nepal or Bhutan may also be considered subject to Central Government permission.

Post-wise Educational Qualification

Post Name Educational & Professional Qualification
Constable (Veterinary) 10th / Matriculation pass with Science as main subject from a recognised board and one year experience in treatment of different species of animals in a recognised veterinary hospital.
Constable (Driver) – Male Matriculation or equivalent from a recognised board and must possess a valid Heavy Vehicle Driving Licence.
Constables (Gardener, Water Carrier, Cobbler, Tailor, Washerman, Barber, Waiter) Matriculation or equivalent plus either (i) two years work experience in the respective trade, or (ii) one year certificate course from a recognised ITI/Vocational Institute with at least one year experience in the trade, or (iii) two years diploma from a recognised ITI in the trade or similar trade; must qualify trade test; multi‑skilled candidates will be given preference.
Constable (Carpenter) Matriculation or equivalent and either (i) two years work experience in the trade, or (ii) one year ITI/Vocational certificate with one year experience, or (iii) two years ITI diploma in the trade; must qualify trade test.
Constable (Nursing Orderly) 10th pass from a recognised board, First Aid certificate from St. John’s Ambulance Organisation or Red Cross Society of India, and at least one year working experience as Nursing Orderly in a minimum 50‑bedded hospital/institution recognised by Central/State Government.

Candidates who are appearing in examinations, awaiting results or not in possession of the required educational/professional certificates on or before the last date of application should not apply for SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Recruitment 2026.

SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Age Limit 2026

  • The crucial date for determining age and all eligibility criteria is the last date for receipt of online applications 
Post / Category Age Limit
Constable (Veterinary) 18 to 25 years
Constable (Driver) – Male 21 to 27 years
Constable (Gardener, Water Carrier, Cobbler, Tailor, Washerman, Barber, Waiter) 18 to 23 years
Constable (Carpenter) 18 to 25 years
Constable (Nursing Orderly) 18 to 27 years
SC / ST 5 years relaxation on upper age limit
OBC (NCL) 3 years relaxation on upper age limit
Ex-Servicemen 3 years relaxation after deduction of military service rendered from actual age
Other eligible categories (Central Govt employees, J&K/Ladakh domiciles, dependents of 1984 riot victims etc.) Age relaxation as per Government of India rules and notification conditions

SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Application Fee 2026

  • For UR / EWS / OBC candidates: ₹100/- (non-refundable examination fee) to be paid through net banking, credit card, debit card, UPI or other online modes as available on the SSB recruitment portal.
  • For SC / ST / Ex-Servicemen / Female candidates: No fee (Exempted) as per SSB recruitment rules.
  • Applications submitted without successful online fee payment (where applicable) will be rejected, so candidates must ensure completion of fee payment and retain proof of transaction.

SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Selection Process 2026

  • Physical Efficiency Test (PET): For Constables (Veterinary, Driver, Gardener, Water Carrier, Cobbler, Tailor, Washerman, Barber, Waiter, Carpenter & Nursing Orderly), PET consists of 4.8 km race in 24 minutes for male candidates and 2.4 km race in 18 minutes for female candidates; Ex-Servicemen are exempted from PET but must appear for other stages.
  • Physical Standard Test (PST): Candidates who qualify PET will undergo PST where height, chest (for male) and weight are measured as per category/region-wise standards; candidates not meeting minimum standards will be rejected.
  • Written Examination: Objective-type written test as per SSB pattern covering General Knowledge, Elementary Mathematics, Reasoning and Trade-related subjects; detailed scheme and syllabus will be notified on the SSB website.
  • Trade/Skill Test: Practical trade test for Tradesman posts and driving/maintenance test for Constable (Driver) to assess hands-on skills; qualifying in nature as per prescribed criteria.
  • Documentation & Medical Examination: Verification of original documents, certificates, category and age proofs followed by medical examination as per SSB medical standards; only medically fit candidates will be considered for final selection.

SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Exam Pattern 2026

The SSB Constable written examination for Tradesman, Driver, Veterinary and other Constable posts will consist of objective-type questions from General Knowledge, Elementary Mathematics, General Reasoning and Trade-related subjects as per the SSB prescribed syllabus. Detailed exam pattern, number of questions, marking scheme and language of question paper will be published in the detailed recruitment notification and on the official SSB website, and candidates should refer to the latest scheme before starting preparation.

SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Salary 2026

Pay Component Details
Constable (All Trades including Driver, Veterinary, Nursing Orderly) Pay Level-3 of 7th CPC pay matrix with basic pay ₹21,700–69,100 plus applicable Dearness Allowance, Ration Money Allowance, House Rent Allowance and other admissible allowances.
Allowances & Benefits Central government benefits including DA, HRA (or government accommodation where admissible), Ration Money, medical facilities, leave, uniforms and other allowances as per SSB/GoI rules.
Pension Scheme Candidates will be covered under Defined Contributory Pension Scheme / Unified Pension Scheme as applicable to new entrants in Central Government service.

SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Documents Required

Candidates will be required to upload scanned copies of documents at the time of online application and produce originals during documentation and medical examination.

  • Recent passport size colour photograph and signature as per prescribed size and format.
  • Matriculation / 10th pass certificate and mark sheet as proof of date of birth and educational qualification.
  • ITI certificates, trade experience certificates, Heavy Vehicle Driving Licence (for Driver post) and First Aid certificate (for Nursing Orderly) as applicable.
  • Caste certificate for SC / ST / OBC (NCL), EWS certificate, issued in prescribed Government of India format, if applicable.
  • Domicile certificate, Ex-Servicemen discharge certificate, NOC from employer for Govt/PSU employees and other supporting documents wherever claimed.

How to Apply for SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Recruitment 2026

  • Candidates must apply online only through the SSB recruitment portal by visiting https://recruitment.ssb.gov.in/ or the link available at https://ssb.gov.in/.
  • Open the Constable (Tradesman & Driver) recruitment link and read the detailed advertisement carefully to ensure that you meet all eligibility conditions.
  • Register with a valid mobile number and email ID, then log in and fill all required details such as personal information, category, address, educational qualifications and trade details exactly as per certificates.
  • Upload scanned copies of recent passport-size photograph, signature and relevant documents/certificates in the prescribed format and size.
  • Pay the examination fee (if applicable) online through net banking, credit card, debit card or UPI and ensure successful transaction before final submission.
  • Preview the filled application form carefully, correct any mistakes if allowed, and then submit the form online on or before 04 May 2026.
  • After successful submission, download and take a printout of the final submitted application form and fee receipt for future reference.

Important Instructions for Candidates

  • Only eligible candidates should apply and must carefully go through all provisions and requirements in the official notification to ensure they are eligible for the post applied for; candidature will be cancelled at any stage if found ineligible.
  • Candidates must bring all original documents such as Matriculation certificate, trade/ITI certificates, experience certificates, category certificates along with self-attested photocopies at the time of documentation, failing which their candidature will be rejected.
  • Name, father’s name and date of birth should exactly match the Matriculation certificate; any change must be supported by valid documents, otherwise candidature is liable to be rejected.
  • Government/Semi-Government/PSU employees should apply through proper channel and must produce No Objection Certificate from their employer at the time of documentation.
  • Falsification or forgery of documents to mislead the recruitment board will invite legal action, cancellation of candidature and debarment from future SSB examinations.
  • Admit cards/call letters for PET, PST, written exam, trade test and documentation will be uploaded on SSB official website only, and candidates must regularly visit recruitment.ssb.gov.in and ssb.gov.in for updates.
  • Mere qualifying all prescribed tests does not confer any right to final selection; the decision of the SSB Recruitment Board will be final and binding.
  • The DG, SSB reserves absolute discretion to cancel, postpone or make changes in the recruitment process without assigning any reason.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The Software Technology Parks of India (STPI) has officially announced the Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026 with 15 vacancies. The detailed notification was published on 22nd April 2026 at stpi.in
. Candidates who meet the age criteria of 18 to 32 years and hold B.Tech/B.E., Diploma, B.Com, or B.A. qualifications can now submit their online applications from 22nd April 2026 to 11th May 2026. Direct links to download the notification and apply online are shared below.

STPI Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Software Technology Parks of India (STPI) has officially published the advertisement (Advt. No. 7(1)/I/STPI-HQ/2026-27, dated 22.04.2026) inviting online applications from eligible Degree/Diploma holders for Apprentice Trainee positions under the Apprenticeship Act, 1961 (as amended from time to time). The official notification contains complete details including discipline-wise and location-wise vacancy breakup, age limits, monthly stipend, eligibility conditions, selection process, NATS portal registration instructions, and reservation provisions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification carefully before applying. Applications received after the last date of 11/05/2026 will not be considered. For any updates or corrigendum, candidates must regularly check STPI's official website www.stpi.in under the Career section.

  • Download STPI Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF (Advt. No. 7(1)/I/STPI-HQ/2026-27) – www.stpi.in

STPI Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organisation Name Software Technology Parks of India (STPI)
Under Ministry Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology (MeitY), Govt. of India
Advertisement No. 7(1)/I/STPI-HQ/2026-27 dated 22.04.2026
Post Name Apprentice Trainee
Total Vacancies 15 Posts
Training Duration One Year
Application Mode Online only (through STPI Web Portal – www.stpi.in, Career Section)
Last Date to Apply Online 11/05/2026
Age Cut-off Date 11/05/2026
NATS Portal Registration Mandatory before applying on STPI portal – https://nats.education.gov.in
Official Website www.stpi.in

STPI Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Advertisement Date 22.04.2026
Age Eligibility Cut-off Date 11/05/2026
Last Date for Online Application 11/05/2026
NATS Portal Registration Must be completed before applying on STPI portal (https://nats.education.gov.in)

STPI Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026 Vacancy Details

A total of 15 Apprentice Trainee vacancies have been announced across two qualification categories - 8 posts for B.Tech/B.E. degree holders and 7 posts for Diploma / B.Com / B.A. holders. Vacancies are spread across multiple STPI centres in Bengaluru, Bhubaneswar, Chennai, Guwahati, Gurugram, Hyderabad, Noida, Mohali, and Pune.

Reservation is as per Government of India rules - out of the 15 vacancies, preference will be given to 3-OBC, 2-SC, and 1-ST candidates. Candidates must carefully apply for the correct discipline and preferred STPI centre location as indicated in the notification.

Discipline-wise and Location-wise Vacancy Breakup

Sl. No. Degree / Diploma / Discipline Total Vacancies Location of Vacancies in STPI Centres
1 B.Tech / B.E. in: Electronics / Electricals / Electronics & Communications / Telecommunications / Computer Science / Information Technology 8 02 – Bengaluru | 01 – Bhubaneswar | 01 – Chennai | 01 – Guwahati | 01 – Gurugram | 01 – Hyderabad | 01 – Noida
2 Diploma in: Electronics / Electricals / Electronics & Communications / Telecommunications / Computer Science / Information Technology
OR Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com) / Bachelor of Arts (B.A.)		 7 03 – Bhubaneswar | 01 – Mohali | 01 – Noida | 01 – Pune | 01 – Hyderabad
Total 15  

Reservation Preference (out of 15 vacancies)

Category Preference (Posts)
OBC 3
SC 2
ST 1

STPI Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria

Sl. No. Qualification Category Eligible Disciplines Additional Conditions
1 B.Tech / B.E. (Degree) Electronics / Electricals / Electronics & Communications / Telecommunications / Computer Science / Information Technology Must have completed qualification in April 2021 or later. Should not have previously undergone apprenticeship.
2 Diploma (Technical) Electronics / Electricals / Electronics & Communications / Telecommunications / Computer Science / Information Technology Must have completed qualification in April 2021 or later. Should not have previously undergone apprenticeship.
2 B.Com / B.A. (Non-Technical Degree) Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com) / Bachelor of Arts (B.A.) – any stream Must have completed qualification in April 2021 or later. Should not have previously undergone apprenticeship.

STPI Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026 Age Limit

The minimum age for all candidates is 18 years as on 11/05/2026. The maximum age limit differs by category - Unreserved (UR/General) candidates must not exceed 27 years, OBC candidates must not exceed 30 years, and SC/ST candidates must not exceed 32 years, all calculated as on 11/05/2026. Age relaxation is in line with Government of India rules for reserved categories. 

Category Minimum Age Maximum Age (as on 11/05/2026)
Unreserved (UR / General) 18 years 27 years
OBC 18 years 30 years
SC / ST 18 years 32 years

STPI Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026 Application Fee

The official notification does not mention any application fee for applying for STPI Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026. The online application through STPI's web portal (www.stpi.in) is free of cost. Similarly, registration on the NATS Portal (https://nats.education.gov.in) is also free. Candidates are advised to apply only through the official portals and not through any third-party website or agency. Be cautious of any individual or platform demanding a fee for application processing or selection facilitation - STPI does not charge any fee for this recruitment.

All Categories NIL (No application fee)

STPI Apprentice Trainee Stipend 2026

Selected Apprentice Trainees will receive a monthly stipend as per the qualification category. B.Tech/B.E. degree holders will receive Rs. 15,000/- per month, Diploma holders will receive Rs. 10,900/- per month, and B.A./B.Com degree holders will receive Rs. 12,300/- per month.

The stipend will be facilitated through the Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) mechanism - the Government's admissible share of the stipend will be directly credited to the apprentice's bank account through the NATS portal after successful DBT registration.

Candidates must ensure their bank account details (account number, IFSC code, bank name) are accurately entered during NATS portal registration, as any error may delay or prevent stipend disbursement. This is a training stipend and not a salary - no other allowances or service benefits are associated with the apprenticeship engagement.

Qualification Category Monthly Stipend Payment Mode
B.Tech / B.E. (Degree) Rs. 15,000/- Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) via NATS Portal
Diploma (Technical) Rs. 10,900/- Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) via NATS Portal
B.A. / B.Com Rs. 12,300/- Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) via NATS Portal

STPI Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026 Selection Process

Stage Details
Stage 1: NATS Portal Registration Candidates must register on https://nats.education.gov.in and obtain a NATS Enrolment ID before applying. Bank details must be entered for DBT stipend processing.
Stage 2: Online Application on STPI Portal Apply online on www.stpi.in (Career Section) on or before 11/05/2026. Enter the NATS Enrolment ID in the application form.
Stage 3: Shortlisting Applications screened based on eligibility criteria - qualification, discipline, age, and NATS registration.
Stage 4: Interview Shortlisted candidates called for interview. Final selection based on marks obtained in the interview.

How to Apply for STPI Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026

The application process is entirely online - only online applications through STPI's web portal will be accepted; no offline, postal, or email applications will be considered. Before applying on the STPI portal, candidates must mandatorily register on the NATS Portal (https://nats.education.gov.in) and obtain their unique NATS Enrolment ID, which is required to be entered in the STPI online application form.

Keep all educational certificates, identity documents, and bank account details ready before starting the NATS registration. After successful NATS registration, visit STPI's official website www.stpi.in, go to the Career Section, and complete the online application before 11/05/2026. Candidates are advised to apply well in advance and not wait until the last date to avoid technical difficulties.

  • Step 1: Register on the NATS Portal at https://nats.education.gov.in and obtain your unique NATS Enrolment ID (refer to Annexure A in the notification for step-by-step instructions).
  • Step 2: Visit STPI's official website www.stpi.in → Career Section.
  • Step 3: Fill in the online application form with all required details including the NATS Enrolment ID.
  • Step 4: Upload required documents as specified in the application form.
  • Step 5: Submit the online application on or before 11/05/2026.
  • Step 6: Take a printout of the submitted application form for future reference.

NATS Portal Registration Guide for STPI Apprentice Trainee 2026

Registration on the NATS Portal (National Apprenticeship Training Scheme - https://nats.education.gov.in) is mandatory for all candidates before applying on the STPI portal. The NATS Enrolment ID generated after registration must be entered in the STPI online application - applications without this ID will not be accepted.

The stipend will also be processed through NATS via DBT, so accurate bank account details must be provided during registration. A step-by-step guide (Annexure A) is included in the official notification. The key steps are summarised below for quick reference.

Step Action Details
Step 1 Visit NATS Portal Go to https://nats.education.gov.in → Click on "Student" in the top menu bar
Step 2 Enrol as a Student Click "Enroll" under Student section → Read eligibility criteria → Click "Yes" if eligible → Proceed to Enrollment Form
Step 3 Fill Personal Information Enter name, date of birth, gender, email, mobile number → Create login ID and password → Enter Aadhaar number and other identification details
Step 4 Fill Educational Details Select highest qualification (Diploma / Graduate) → Enter institution name, year of passing, percentage/CGPA
Step 5 Fill Communication & Bank Details Enter present and permanent address → Enter bank account number, IFSC code, and bank name (for DBT stipend processing)
Step 6 Review and Submit Review all details carefully → Submit the registration form → Unique NATS Enrolment ID generated and sent to registered email ID

Important Instructions for Candidates

  • Only online applications through www.stpi.in (Career Section) will be accepted.
  • NATS Portal registration at https://nats.education.gov.in is mandatory before applying on STPI portal.
  • Qualification must have been completed in April 2021 or thereafter.
  • Candidates who have already completed apprenticeship anywhere are not eligible.
  • Reservation as per Govt. of India rules: preference to 3-OBC, 2-SC, 1-ST out of 15 posts.
  • Stipend paid through DBT via NATS portal - ensure bank details are correctly entered during NATS registration.
  • Applications received after 11/05/2026 will not be considered under any circumstances.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Bank of Baroda (BOB) has published the Customer Service Associate (Clerical Cadre) Notification 2026 on 22nd April 2026. The recruitment drive aims to fill 30 Customer Service Associate positions for meritorious sportspersons. Candidates within the age range of 20 to 28 years and holding a Degree in any discipline are eligible to apply online at www.bankofbaroda.bank.in. The application process will be active from 22nd April 2026 to 12th May 2026, and candidates can easily access the notification PDF and apply link below.

Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Bank of Baroda has officially published the advertisement (BOB/HRM/REC/ADVT/2026/09) inviting applications from meritorious sportspersons for engagement as Customer Service Associate in the Clerical Cadre on a regular basis. The official notification PDF contains complete details including vacancy breakup, eligibility criteria, selection process, pay scale, application fee, document requirements, and all prescribed annexure forms. Candidates are strongly advised to download and read the full notification before filling the online application form to ensure they meet all eligibility conditions and avoid rejection at any stage. The notification is hosted on the official Bank of Baroda website and can be accessed free of cost.

  • Download Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF (Advt. No. BOB/HRM/REC/ADVT/2026/09)

Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organisation Name Bank of Baroda
Advertisement No. BOB/HRM/REC/ADVT/2026/09
Post Name Customer Service Associate (Clerical Cadre) – Regular Basis
Total Vacancies 30 Posts (24 Male + 6 Female)
Sports Disciplines Cricket, Kabaddi, Table Tennis, Football
Application Mode Online only
Application Start Date 22.04.2026
Last Date to Apply 12.05.2026
Eligibility Cut-Off Date 01.04.2026
Official Website www.bankofbaroda.bank.in

Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Online Application Registration Starts 22.04.2026
Last Date for Submission of Application & Payment of Fees 12.05.2026
Eligibility Cut-Off Date 01.04.2026
Field Trial Date To be notified on Bank's website

Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026 Vacancy Details

A total of 30 vacancies have been announced across 4 sports disciplines - Cricket, Kabaddi, Table Tennis, and Football - spread across posting locations in Delhi, Mumbai, and Chennai. Out of 30 posts, 24 are for Male candidates and 6 for Female candidates.

Each vacancy carries a specific role/specialisation requirement (e.g., Batsman, Fast Bowler, Goalkeeper, Midfielder, etc.), and candidates must ensure they apply only for the role matching their sports specialisation. The Bank reserves the right to modify the number of vacancies and their breakup as per its requirement. There is no reservation for SC/ST/OBC/EWS categories in this recruitment.

Recruitment of Sportspersons: Breakup of Vacancies

Sport Discipline Location Gender Vacancies Role (Specialization)
Cricket Delhi Male 4 01 Batsman, 01 Spinner Allrounder, 01 Fast Bowler, 01 Fast Bowler Allrounder
Mumbai Male 5 01 Batsman, 01 Allrounder, 02 Fast Bowlers, 01 Left Arm Spinner
Kabaddi Mumbai Male 6 01 Right Corner (Defender), 01 Left Centre (Defender), 01 Left Corner (Defender), 01 Right Raider, 01 Right Centre (Defender), 01 Left Raider

Table Tennis Chennai Male 1 01 Player (Singles & Doubles)
Mumbai Female 1 01 Singles Player
Football Mumbai Male 8 01 Goalkeeper, 02 Defenders, 03 Midfielders, 02 Forwards
Total 30 24 Male + 6 Female

Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria

Eligibility Criteria: Customer Service Associate (30 Posts)

Position Vacancies Education Qualification Sports Qualifications
Customer Service Associate 30 Degree in any discipline from a recognised University or any equivalent qualifications recognised as such by the Central Government Should have represented a State / U.T. / the country in National / International competition; OR Medal winner in Junior National Championships; OR Medal winner in Khelo India Youth Games (age category above 18 years) / Khelo India Winter Games / Khelo India Para Games; OR Medal winner in Khelo India University Games; OR Medal winner in School Games Federation of India (SGFI); OR Represented University in Inter-University Tournaments; OR Represented State/U.T. School Team in National Sports/Games for Schools conducted by All India School Games Federation.

*Relaxation in upper age limit up to a maximum of five years to sportspersons considered meritorious, as per DOPT, Govt of India O.M. No. DOPT-1720781414772 dated 04.03.2020. Ten years relaxation in the case of SC/ST sportspersons.

Authority for Awarding Sports Certificate

S.N. Competition Authority for Awarding Certificate
1 International Competition Secretary of National Federation of the Game concerned.
2 National Competition Secretary of the National Federation or Secretary of the State / U.T. Association of the Game concerned.
3 Inter-University Tournaments Dean of Sports or other officer in overall charge of Sports of the University concerned.
4 National / Sports / Games for Schools Director or Additional / Joint or Deputy Director in overall charge of sports / games for Schools in the Directorate of Public Instructions / Education of the State.
5 Khelo India University Games / Khelo India Youth Games / Khelo India Winter Games / Khelo India Para Games Officer designated / Officer in charge from Sports Authority of India (SAI).

Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026 Age Limit

The minimum age to apply is 20 years and the maximum age is 28 years for General/EWS category candidates, calculated as on the cut-off date of 01.04.2026. 

S No Category Age Relaxation Relaxation to Meritorious Sportspersons as per DOPT guidelines* Total Age Relaxation*
1 General / EWS Nil 5 Years 5 Years
2 Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe 5 years 5 years 10 years
3 Other Backward Classes (NCL) 3 years 5 years 8 years

*This concession will be available only to those meritorious sportspersons who satisfy all other eligibility criteria and furnish a certificate in the form and from an authority prescribed as per the guidelines issued by the Government from time to time.

Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026 Application Fee

The application fee is non-refundable and must be paid online at the time of registration regardless of whether the candidate is shortlisted for Field Trials or not. General, EWS, and OBC candidates are required to pay ₹850/- (inclusive of GST) plus applicable payment gateway charges. SC, ST, ESM/DESM (Ex-Servicemen/Disabled Ex-Servicemen), and Women candidates are required to pay ₹175/- (intimation charges only, inclusive of GST) plus applicable payment gateway charges.

Payment can be made through Debit Card, Credit Card, Internet Banking, UPI, or any other available online payment mode. Transaction charges, if any, are to be borne by the candidate. Once fee payment is completed successfully, an e-receipt and application form will be generated, which should be printed and retained. The Bank will not entertain any refund requests under any circumstances.

Category Application Fee
General / EWS / OBC ₹850/- (Inclusive of GST) + Payment Gateway Charges
SC / ST / ESM / DESM / Women ₹175/- (Inclusive of GST) + Payment Gateway Charges

Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026 Pay Scale & Benefits

Selected candidates will be placed in the clerical cadre pay scale as applicable to Customer Service Associates in Bank of Baroda. The starting basic pay is ₹24,050/- per month and the scale progresses through multiple incremental stages over a period of 20 years, reaching up to ₹64,480/-. Increments are applied at different rates at various stages of the pay scale as detailed below. 

Pay Scale (as amended from time to time)
24050 – 1340/3 – 28070 – 1650/3 – 33020 – 2000/4 – 41020 – 2340/7 – 57400 – 4400/1 – 61800 – 2680/1 – 64480 (20 years)

Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026 Selection Process

The selection process consists of two main stages: Shortlisting/Screening of applications followed by Field Trials for shortlisted candidates. In the first stage, applications will be screened against the eligibility criteria including educational qualifications, relevant sports experience, domain expertise, and suitability. Meeting the minimum eligibility criteria does not automatically entitle a candidate to be called for Field Trials - the Bank may call candidates in a ratio as deemed appropriate at its sole discretion. The shortlisting criteria are internal and confidential and will not be shared with candidates.

Shortlisted candidates will be called for Field Trials at a designated venue, the evaluation criteria and qualifying cut-off for which will be determined solely by the Bank. No requests for change of centre, venue, date, or time for Field Trials will be entertained. The Bank's decision on shortlisting and selection at all stages is final and binding. Selected candidates must be Active Sportspersons at the time of joining and fit enough to participate in future tournaments. All selected sportspersons will be required to sign a written undertaking to play for the Bank's team whenever required.

Stage Details
Stage 1: Shortlisting / Screening Applications screened based on eligibility criteria, sports experience, qualifications, and suitability as determined by the Bank. Criteria are internal and confidential.
Stage 2: Field Trials Shortlisted candidates called for Field Trials at designated venue. Evaluation criteria and qualifying cut-off determined solely by the Bank.
Stage 3: Document Verification Original documents to be produced at the time of Field Trials / as called by the Bank. Failure to produce documents will debar candidature.

Obligations of Candidates Recruited as Sportspersons

Obligation Details
Playing for the Bank Sportsperson must play for the Bank / Bank's team whenever and wherever necessary.
Permission for External Events Prior permission required to participate in State / National / International events for other organisations. Bank reserves the right to withhold permission.
Misconduct Refusal to play for the Bank or playing elsewhere without permission will be treated as misconduct and attract disciplinary proceedings.
Written Undertaking All recruited sportspersons must give a written undertaking at the time of appointment.

How to Apply for Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026

The application process is entirely online - no other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates must visit the Bank's official website www.bankofbaroda.bank.in and apply through the Careers → Current Opportunities section. Before starting the application, candidates must ensure they have a valid personal email ID and active mobile number, a scanned passport-size photograph (20–50 KB, JPG format), a scanned signature in black ink on white paper (10–20 KB, JPG format), and scanned PDF copies of all required documents within the specified file sizes (maximum 500 KB per file).

Candidates are advised to fill the application with utmost care as no changes will be permitted after clicking the SUBMIT button. All details including name, caste, category, date of birth, mobile number, and email ID will be treated as final. Candidates must not submit more than one application; in case of multiple submissions, only the last valid completed application will be retained and fees for earlier applications will be forfeited. A printout of the system-generated application form and e-receipt should be retained for future reference.

  • Visit: www.bankofbaroda.bank.in → Careers → Current Opportunities
  • Register online and fill the application form carefully in all fields.
  • Upload scanned photograph, signature, and all required documents as per specifications in Annexure I.
  • Pay the application fee online via Debit Card / Credit Card / Internet Banking / UPI.
  • On successful payment, download and retain the e-receipt and application form printout.
  • Produce original documents at the time of Field Trials as per the document list in Section 10.4 of the notification.

Documents Required for Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026

S.No. Document
i Printout of the valid Call Letter
ii Valid system-generated printout of the online application form
iii Proof of Date of Birth (Birth Certificate / SSLC / Std. X Marksheet or Certificate with DOB)
iv Photo Identity Proof (PAN Card / Passport / Driving Licence / Voter's Card / Bank Passbook with photo / Aadhar Card / Employee ID / Gazetted Officer ID)
v Certificate(s) / Awards / Medals fulfilling Sports Qualification Eligibility Criteria
vi Individual Semester/Year-wise Mark Sheets & Certificates for educational qualifications including final degree/diploma certificate
vii Caste Certificate in prescribed format (for SC / ST / OBC category candidates) - as per Annexure II
viii OBC certificate with Non-Creamy Layer clause (valid within one year as on date of advertisement)
ix EWS Income & Asset Certificate (for EWS candidates) - as per prescribed format
x Service / Discharge Book with pension payment order and rank proof (for Ex-Servicemen)
xi No Objection Certificate from employer (for candidates serving in Govt / PSU / Nationalised Bank)
xii Relevant documents in support of work experience (appointment letter, salary slip, relieving letter, etc.) wherever applicable
xiii Disability Certificate (Form I / II / III as applicable) for PWD candidates

Important Instructions for Candidates

Candidates must read the full official notification carefully before filling the application. All eligibility criteria are to be met as on the cut-off date of 01.04.2026. Change of category is not permitted at any stage after submission of the online application. Candidates should not furnish false, fabricated, or tampered information at any stage - doing so will result in disqualification or termination even after joining. Any canvassing or creating undue influence will lead to disqualification. All legal proceedings arising from this recruitment can only be instituted at courts in Mumbai. Signatures must remain consistent across all documents and correspondence throughout the recruitment process. Selected candidates will be governed by the Bank's service conditions including provisions related to probation (6 months), confirmation, disciplinary rules, and execution of service bonds. Candidates with adverse credit history, criminal cases, or adverse reports from CIBIL or other credit agencies will be ineligible. The Bank reserves the right to reject, modify, increase/decrease vacancies, or cancel the recruitment process at any stage without assigning any reason.

  • Online application once submitted cannot be modified. Fill with utmost care.
  • Keep email ID and mobile number active till completion of the recruitment process.
  • No request for change of Field Trial centre, venue, date or time will be entertained.
  • Maintain a minimum CIBIL score of 680 at the time of joining.
  • Probation period is 6 months from the date of joining.
  • All announcements / corrigendum will be posted only on the Bank's official website.
  • No separate communication will be sent to candidates who are not shortlisted or not selected.

No. NABCONS/CO-HR/001/PBCS/2026-27) for engagement of Middle Level Consultants and Junior Level Consultants for handling Tribal Development Projects under NABARD's Farm Sector Development Department. NABCONS TDF Consultant Recruitment 2026 Age Limit

The age criteria for NABCONS TDF Consultant Recruitment 2026 is as on the date of application. For the post of Middle Level Consultant, the minimum age is 24 years and the maximum age is 61 years. There is no application fee mentioned in the official notification for NABCONS TDF Consultant Recruitment 2026. NABCONS TDF Consultant Recruitment 2026 Salary / Remuneration

Selected candidates engaged as consultants at NABCONS for Tribal Development Projects will be paid a consolidated monthly remuneration under Section 192 of the Income Tax Act, based on experience, educational qualification, and overall suitability. The remuneration is inclusive of all statutory deductions that NABCONS may be required to make on behalf of the contracted staff, and will be released after making statutory deductions. Tax will be deducted at source as per the Income Tax Act and Rules.

There will be an annual revision in the remuneration by 5% within the range stated, which shall be considered after one year of satisfactory service. Candidates are not eligible for any other facilities or allowances such as leave encashment, performance bonus, or leave travel concession beyond what is mentioned in the notification.

NABCONS TDF Consultant Recruitment 2026 Selection Process

The selection process for NABCONS TDF Consultant Recruitment 2026 consists of shortlisting of candidates based on their application details, followed by a Personal Interview. Only shortlisted candidates will be called for the interview. NABCONS will shortlist candidates based on preliminary screening with reference to qualification, suitability, and experience.

Merely satisfying the eligibility criteria does not entitle a candidate to be called for the interview.

At the time of the interview, candidates may also be assessed for their proficiency in MS Office and data management. Self-attested copies of educational qualifications and experience certificates must be compulsorily submitted at the time of the interview, and original documents will be required for verification. The interview may be held in person or through virtual mode as required by NABCONS.

The location for the interview will be indicated in the call letter. Any cost incurred by candidates for attending the interview will not be reimbursed by NABCONS. The final appointment will be based on the decision of the selection committee constituted for the purpose.

Stage Details
Stage 1 - Online Application Screening / Shortlisting Based on qualification, suitability, and experience as mentioned in the online application
Stage 2 - Personal Interview In-person or virtual mode; includes assessment of MS Office and data management proficiency; self-attested and original documents to be produced
Final Appointment Based on decision of selection committee; subject to medical fitness

How to Apply for NABCONS TDF Consultant Recruitment 2026

Eligible candidates must apply ONLY ONLINE through the official NABCONS website at www.nabcons.com. No other mode of submission of application will be accepted. The online application window is open from 20 April 2026 to 30 April 2026. Candidates must apply for only one state/location at a time. Separate online application forms are available for Middle Level Consultant and Junior Level Consultant positions respectively. Before applying, candidates must read all the instructions carefully and ensure they fulfil all the eligibility criteria.

NABCONS will admit candidates on the basis of information furnished in the online application and will verify eligibility at the stage of interview/joining. If at any stage any information furnished is found false or incorrect, the candidature will be cancelled. Candidates must submit the declaration in the Google form with respect to their educational qualification and experience. Self-attested copies of educational qualifications and experience certificates must be brought to the interview.

  • Click on the appropriate application link for Middle Level Consultant or Junior Level Consultant - TDF
  • Read all instructions carefully and fill in all relevant details accurately
  • Apply for only one state/location per application
  • Submit the online application form before 30 April 2026
  • Keep a copy of the submitted application for reference
  • Bring self-attested copies and originals of all documents at the time of interview

NABCONS TDF Consultant Recruitment 2026 Application Form

The online application form for NABCONS TDF Consultant Recruitment 2026 is available exclusively on the official NABCONS website at www.nabcons.com. Separate application forms are available for the post of Middle Level Consultant and Junior Level Consultant respectively. Under no circumstances will applications by hand or any other mode be entertained. Only the application submitted through the online mode provided in this advertisement will be accepted. Candidates applying for NABCONS TDF Consultant Recruitment 2026 must carefully read and comply with all instructions mentioned in the official notification. 📄 Complete Details & Notification

The Punjab National Bank (PNB) has officially announced the Specialist Officer (Engineer) Recruitment 2026 with 30 openings. The detailed notification was published on 20 April 2026 at pnb.bank.in. Candidates who meet the age criteria of 20 to 30 years and hold B.E./B.Tech in Civil / Electrical / Mechanical Engineering with 60% marks along with minimum 1 year post-qualification experience in the relevant field can now submit their online applications from 21 April 2026 to 05 May 2026. Direct links to download the notification and apply online are shared below.

PNB Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Punjab National Bank has officially released the recruitment notification dated 20 April 2026 for the Recruitment of 30 Specialist Officers (Engineer) in JMG Scale I on direct recruitment basis. The notification contains complete details regarding vacancy distribution, eligibility criteria, age limit, post-qualification work experience, job roles, selection procedure, examination centres, application fee, and general instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to download and read the official notification thoroughly before filling out and submitting their online application at https://pnb.bank.in/. Requests for change of category or any other details will not be permitted after submission of the online application form. All updates and further announcements will be published exclusively on the official PNB website.

  • Download PNB Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

PNB Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 Overview
