PNB Specialist Officer Vacancy 2026

A total of 30 vacancies have been announced for Specialist Officer (Engineer) posts in JMG Scale I across three disciplines - Civil Engineering (21 posts), Electrical Engineering (7 posts), and Mechanical Engineering (2 posts). 

Specialist Officer (Engineer) Vacancy Distribution (30 Posts)

Post Name Total Vacancy
Officer - Civil Engineer (JMG Scale I) 21
Officer - Electrical Engineer (JMG Scale I) 7
Officer - Mechanical Engineer (JMG Scale I) 2
Total 30

PNB Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria

Post Educational Qualification Post-Qualification Experience
Officer - Civil Engineer B.E./B.Tech in Civil Engineering from a recognised University with 60% marks (or equivalent recognised by Govt. of India) Minimum 1 year post-qualification experience in Civil Engineering - Construction/Renovation/Retrofitting/Interior Furnishing/Maintenance of Commercial/Residential/Institutional/Industrial/Administrative buildings; monitoring civil work of construction projects; preparation of estimates; checking of contractor bills
Officer - Electrical Engineer B.E./B.Tech in Electrical Engineering from a recognised University with 60% marks (or equivalent recognised by Govt. of India) Minimum 1 year post-qualification experience in Electrical Engineering - Construction/Renovation/Retrofitting/Interior Furnishing/Maintenance of Commercial/Residential/Institutional/Industrial/Administrative buildings; OR experience in installation/handling/maintenance of electro-mechanical equipment (UPS, Generators, Energy Saving Devices, Control Panels, HT-LT, Switch Gears, Cabling, Water Pumps, Air Conditioning Equipment, Lifts, etc.)
Officer - Mechanical Engineer B.E./B.Tech in Mechanical Engineering from a recognised University with 60% marks (or equivalent recognised by Govt. of India) Minimum 1 year post-qualification experience in Mechanical Engineering - Construction/Renovation/Retrofitting/Interior Furnishing/Maintenance of Commercial/Residential/Institutional/Industrial/Administrative buildings; AND experience in installation/handling/maintenance of electro-mechanical equipment (UPS, Generators, Energy Saving Devices, Control Panels, HT-LT, Switch Gears, Cabling, Water Pumps, Air Conditioning Equipment, Lifts, etc.)

PNB Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 Age Limit

The age limit for PNB Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 is calculated as on 01.01.2026. The minimum age is 20 years and the maximum age is 30 years for all three posts. The maximum age limit is applicable to Unreserved (UR) category and Economically Weaker Section (EWS) category candidates. Age relaxation is applicable to reserved category candidates as per Government of India rules. 

Category Age Relaxation
Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe 5 Years
Other Backward Classes (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 Years
Persons with Benchmark Disability (PwBD) 10 Years
Ex-Servicemen / ECOs / SSCOs (with minimum 5 years military service) 5 Years
Persons Affected by 1984 Riots 5 Years

PNB Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay a non-refundable application fee through online mode only. Bank transaction charges for online payment of application fee will have to be borne by the candidate. The fee once paid shall not be refunded under any circumstances, nor can it be held in reserve for any other examination or selection.

A candidate can apply for only one post; in case of multiple applications, only the latest valid and completed application will be retained and the fee paid for other multiple registrations will stand forfeited. Payment can be made using Debit Cards (RuPay/Visa/MasterCard), Credit Cards, Internet Banking, IMPS, Cash Cards/Mobile Wallets, or UPI.

Category Application Fee (per candidate)
SC / ST / PwBD candidates ₹50/- + GST @18% = ₹59/- (only postage charges)
All Others (General / EWS / OBC) ₹1,000/- + GST @18% = ₹1,180/-

PNB Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 Salary / Pay Scale

Selected candidates appointed as Specialist Officers (Engineer) in JMG Scale I at Punjab National Bank will be entitled to pay and allowances as per the Bank's rules applicable to JMG Scale I officers. In addition to the basic pay, selected candidates will be entitled to Dearness Allowance (DA), City Compensatory Allowance (CCA), House Rent Allowance (HRA) or Leased Accommodation, Leave Fare Concession, Medical Insurance, Retirement Benefits, and other perquisites as per Bank rules.

The selected candidates will be required to execute an Indemnity Bond undertaking to serve the Bank for a minimum period of 03 years, or to pay the Bank a sum of ₹3,00,000/- (Rupees Three Lakhs Only) in case they leave before completion of 03 years of active service. Candidates must also maintain a healthy credit history with a minimum CIBIL score of 681 or above at the time of joining the Bank.

Post Scale Allowances & Benefits
Officer - Civil / Electrical / Mechanical Engineer JMG Scale I Basic Pay + DA + CCA + HRA/Leased Accommodation + Leave Fare Concession + Medical Insurance + Retirement Benefits + Other Perquisites as per Bank Rules

PNB Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 Selection Process

The selection process for PNB Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 will be based on an Online Written Test followed by a Personal Interview, or a Personal Interview only, depending upon the number of applications received against each post. The Bank may decide to follow either of two scenarios. Under Scenario 1 (Online Written Test + Interview):

The written test consists of Part I covering Reasoning (25 marks), English Language (25 marks), and Quantitative Aptitude (50 marks) - totalling 100 marks - and Part II covering Professional Knowledge (50 questions, 100 marks), with the entire test to be completed in 120 minutes.

For each wrong answer, one-fourth of the marks assigned will be deducted as penalty. Part II (Professional Knowledge) will be evaluated only for candidates who qualify in each paper of Part I. Candidates who qualify Part I will be shortlisted for interview based on marks in Part II. Personal Interview will carry 50 marks, and candidates must score minimum qualifying marks in the interview to be considered for final selection.

The final merit will be based on aggregate scores of Part II of the Online Written Test and the Interview. Under Scenario 2 (Shortlisting + Interview): Applications will be screened, eligible candidates will be called for a Personal Interview of 50 marks, and final selection will be based solely on interview scores.

Online Written Test Pattern (Scenario 1 - if applicable)

Part Test Name No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration
PART I Reasoning 25 25 120 Minutes
English Language 25 25
Quantitative Aptitude 50 50
PART II Professional Knowledge 50 100

How to Apply for PNB Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026

Eligible candidates must apply online only through the official Punjab National Bank website at https://pnb.bank.in/ under the Recruitment/Career section. No other mode of application will be accepted. Online applications can be submitted from 21 April 2026 to 05 May 2026. Candidates are required to have a valid personal Email ID and mobile number, which must remain active till the completion of the recruitment process.

Before applying, candidates must scan and upload their photograph, signature, left hand thumb impression, handwritten declaration, proof of age, caste/disability certificate, educational qualification certificates, and work experience documents as per the prescribed specifications. After successful registration, a provisional registration number and password will be generated and sent via email and SMS.

Candidates must take a printout of the system-generated online application form and e-receipt after payment and retain it for future reference. They should not send this printout to the Bank. All particulars filled in the online application form - including name, caste, date of birth, post applied for, address, mobile number, email ID, and centre of examination - will be treated as final, and no change or modification will be allowed after submission.

  • Visit the official website https://pnb.bank.in/ under Recruitment/Career section
  • Click on "Click Here for New Registration" and enter basic details to obtain provisional registration number and password
  • Fill in personal, qualification, experience, and category details accurately
  • Upload scanned photograph, signature, left hand thumb impression, handwritten declaration, and all required documents as per prescribed specifications
  • Pay the application fee online through Debit Card/Credit Card/Internet Banking/IMPS/UPI/Mobile Wallets
  • Submit the application after verifying all details; take printout of e-receipt and application form for future reference
  • Do not send the printout to the Bank

PNB Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 Application Form

The online application form for PNB Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 is available exclusively on the official Punjab National Bank website at https://pnb.bank.in/ under the Recruitment/Career section. The form requires candidates to fill in their personal details, educational qualification details, post-qualification work experience, category details, and choice of examination centre.

Candidates must also capture and upload a live photograph via webcam or mobile phone in addition to the scanned passport-size photograph. The handwritten declaration must be written in English only (not in capital letters) by the candidate in their own handwriting on white paper with black ink, and must state: "I, __________ (Name of the candidate), hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required." A candidate can apply for only one post.

No change in any data filled in the online application will be permitted after clicking the Complete Registration button. An application incomplete in any respect - including without proper photograph, signature, left hand thumb impression, handwritten declaration, or failed fee payment - will not be considered valid.

Apply Online at: https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/pnbvpapr26/ (Under Recruitment / Career section)

Important Instructions for Candidates

Candidates applying for PNB Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 must carefully read and comply with all instructions mentioned in the official notification before submitting their application. A candidate can apply for only one post. Candidature at all stages of the recruitment process shall remain provisional, subject to verification of eligibility criteria and other supporting documents.

Merely being shortlisted, appearing for the test/interview, or inclusion in the merit list does not confer any right to appointment. Selected candidates will be required to execute a Service Indemnity Bond undertaking to serve the Bank for a minimum of 03 years or pay ₹3,00,000/- if they leave before completion of 03 years.

Candidates must maintain a minimum CIBIL score of 681 or above at the time of joining. Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification. The Bank reserves the right to cancel the recruitment process partly or entirely at any time without assigning any reason. In case of cancellation, application fees deposited will be refunded.

Any dispute arising out of the recruitment process shall be subject to the sole jurisdiction of the Courts situated at NCT of Delhi. Candidates are advised to possess/acquire a valid Aadhaar card for the process, as the Bank may conduct Online Aadhaar verification at different stages of the selection process.

  • A candidate can apply for only one post; in case of multiple applications, only the latest valid application will be retained.
  • Candidature at all stages is provisional and subject to verification of documents.
  • Candidates who have earlier worked in PNB or its merged/amalgamated entities and were discharged for reasons other than VRS/Resignation are NOT eligible.
  • Candidates with adverse reports regarding character, antecedents, or moral turpitude are NOT eligible.
  • Requests for change of contact number/address/email ID/examination centre/interview centre will not be entertained.
  • Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification.
  • Selected candidates must execute a Service Indemnity Bond for 03 years or pay ₹3,00,000/-.
  • Minimum CIBIL score of 681 or above required at the time of joining.
  • All disputes subject to sole jurisdiction of Courts at NCT of Delhi.
  • Bank reserves the right to cancel/change the recruitment process at any stage without prior notice.

How to Apply for Sarkari Job Online 2026

  1. Find the right Sarkari Job: Use the vacancy table or sector links above on OnlyJobs. Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, or Engineering. Check the last date and eligibility carefully.

  2. Read the official notification: Click on the job listing to open the official notification PDF. Read all eligibility conditions — age limit, educational qualification, experience, category relaxations.

  3. Visit the official recruitment website: Never apply through third-party sites. Use the direct official link provided on OnlyJobs. Look for "Apply Online" or "Register" on the official portal.

  4. Register / Create account: Sign up using your valid email ID and active mobile number. Keep your Aadhaar, bank account details, and email accessible.

  5. Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualifications, category (General/OBC/SC/ST/EWS), address, and other required information accurately.

  6. Upload documents: Scan and upload recent passport-size photograph, signature, 10th certificate (for DOB proof), educational certificates, caste certificate (if applicable). Check file size and format (JPG/PDF as specified).

  7. Pay application fee: Pay via Net Banking, UPI, Debit/Credit Card as applicable. Some categories (SC/ST/PwD/Women) may be exempted from fee. Keep the payment receipt.

  8. Submit and save confirmation: Review all details before final submission. After submitting, download the application printout / PDF confirmation with your registration number. Store it safely for admit card, exam, and future reference.

Why Sarkari Job? Benefits of Government Employment in India

Job Security
Permanent employment — cannot be terminated without due process. Lifelong career stability.
7th Pay Commission Salary
Fixed salary + DA, HRA, TA allowances. DA revised every 6 months. Regular pay revisions.
Medical Benefits
Free or subsidised medical treatment for employee and family under CGHS / state health schemes.
Housing Benefits
Government quarters (where available) or HRA (8–27% of basic pay). No private rent worries.
Pension (NPS / OPS)
Post-retirement monthly pension under NPS or OPS (state-specific). Financial security after service.
Work-Life Balance
Fixed working hours. Paid casual, medical, earned leave. National and state holidays. No overtime culture.
Career Growth
Regular promotions through DPC, ACR-based upgrades, MACP benefits (3 financial upgrades guaranteed).
Children's Education Allowance
Reimbursement of children's school fees (₹2,812/month per child up to 2 children, revised periodically).

Frequently Asked Questions – Sarkari Job 2026

What is Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी)?

Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी) refers to government employment in India — jobs offered by central government departments, state governments, public sector undertakings (PSUs), banks, railways, defence forces, and other government-funded organisations. These jobs offer permanent employment, fixed salary as per 7th Pay Commission, pension, medical benefits, and unmatched job security.

How to find Sarkari Job vacancy 2026 online?

To find online Sarkari Job vacancies in 2026: (1) Visit OnlyJobs daily for updated notifications. (2) Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, PG. (3) Filter by sector — Railway, Banking, SSC, Defence, State PSC, Teaching. (4) Check last dates carefully. (5) Apply through the official department website using the direct link provided. Enable push notifications on OnlyJobs for instant alerts.

What Sarkari Job can I get after 10th pass?

After 10th pass, you can apply for: Railway Group D (RRB), Indian Army Soldier posts, Postal Mail Guard, SSC MTS / Havaldar, Constable GD, Anganwadi Worker/Helper, ITI Apprentice in PSUs (IOCL, HPCL, NTPC), MCL / Coal India posts, and various state government peon/chowkidar posts. Thousands of 10th pass Sarkari Job vacancies open every year.

What is the salary in Sarkari Job 2026?

Sarkari Job salary in 2026 follows the 7th Pay Commission: Group D / MTS: ₹22,000–₹26,000/month. Clerk / Group C: ₹32,000–₹48,000/month. Officer Grade (SSC CGL, Bank PO): ₹47,000–₹85,000/month. IAS/IPS (UPSC): ₹56,100–₹1,44,200 basic pay + DA, HRA, TA, medical and other allowances. State government salaries vary by state pay commission.

Which Bharat Sarkari Job has the most vacancies in 2026?

The largest Bharat Sarkari Job recruitments in 2026 include: Indian Army Agniveer (40,000+ posts), RRB Group D (22,000+ posts), SSC CGL (14,582 posts), SSC MTS (10,000+ posts), UP Police Constable (multiple), Bihar Police (multiple), IBPS Clerk (8,000+ posts), and various state PSC recruitments. Total active sarkari vacancies in India exceed 52,920 as of March 2026.

What is the best website to find Sarkari Job in India?

OnlyJobs is India's most trusted portal for Sarkari Job notifications since 2011, serving over 10 million users. Official direct sources include: ssc.nic.in (SSC), indianrailways.gov.in (Railways), upsc.gov.in (UPSC), ibps.in (Banking), and respective state PSC websites. OnlyJobs aggregates all official notifications in one place with direct apply links.

How to apply for Sarkari Job online in 2026?

To apply for Sarkari Job online: (1) Read official notification PDF. (2) Check eligibility. (3) Visit official recruitment website. (4) Register with email/mobile. (5) Fill application form. (6) Upload photo, signature, and certificates. (7) Pay fee via Net Banking/UPI/Debit card. (8) Submit and lication confirmation. Save the registration number for admit card and future reference.

What are the benefits of Sarkari Job in India?

Key benefits: (1) Lifelong job security. (2) 7th Pay Commission salary + DA revised biannually. (3) HRA (8–27% of basic). (4) Medical benefits (CGHS). (5) Pension under NPS/OPS. (6) Paid leave — casual, earned, medical. (7) Gratuity after 5 years. (8) Children's education allowance. (9) Fixed working hours and work-life balance. (10) Prestige and social recognition in Indian society.

What is the age limit for Sarkari Job?

Age limits vary by post: Group D (18–25 years), SSC CGL (18–32 years), Bank PO (20–30 years), UPSC Civil Services (21–32 years), Defence Agniveer (17.5–23 years). Age relaxations: SC/ST — 5 years, OBC — 3 years, PwD — 10 years, Ex-Servicemen — 3 years (general), Government servants — up to 5 years. Always verify in the official notification for each post.

What is Bharat Sarkari Job?

Bharat Sarkari Job means government job opportunities across the entire country of India (Bharat) — spanning central and state government vacancies across all 28 states and 8 union territories. The term emphasises pan-India coverage. OnlyJobs covers all Bharat Sarkari Job notifications from UPSC, SSC, Railways, Banking, Defence, state PSCs, and all other government departments.

About OnlyJobs 2026

OnlyJobs (Government Jobs) are highly sought-after employment opportunities in India. These positions offer job security, good salary packages, allowances, and retirement benefits. Our platform provides comprehensive information about all types of government vacancies including:

  • Central Government Jobs: UPSC, SSC, Railway, Banking, Defence, PSUs
  • State Government Jobs: State PSCs, Police, Teachers, Healthcare workers
  • Qualification-based Jobs: 10th Pass, 12th Pass, Graduate, Post Graduate, Engineering
  • Category-wise Jobs: General, OBC, SC, ST, EWS, Ex-Servicemen, PwD

Frequently Asked Questions - OnlyJobs 2026

Q1: What is the eligibility for OnlyJobs?

Eligibility varies by position. Generally, candidates need to be Indian citizens aged 18-35 years (with age relaxation for reserved categories). Educational qualifications range from 10th pass to post-graduation depending on the job.

Q2: How to prepare for Government Job exams?

Preparation includes understanding the exam pattern, studying from standard books, practicing previous year papers, taking mock tests, and staying updated with current affairs. Focus on subjects like General Knowledge, Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, and English.

Q3: Which are the best Government Jobs in 2026?

Top government jobs include IAS/IPS (UPSC), Bank PO, SSC CGL, Railway Group A & B, Defence Services, PSU Jobs, State PCS, and Teaching positions. These offer excellent career growth, job security, and benefits.

Q4: What is the application process for OnlyJobs?

The process typically involves: 1) Online registration on the official website, 2) Filling application form, 3) Uploading documents (photo, signature, certificates), 4) Payment of application fee, 5) Downloading admit card, 6) Appearing for examination, 7) Document verification if selected.

Q5: How often are new OnlyJobs notifications released?

New government job notifications are released daily. Major recruitments like UPSC, SSC, IBPS have annual calendars, while state governments and PSUs release notifications throughout the year. We update our portal daily with 795+ active job listings.

Application Fee NIL (most states) NIL (most states) ₹100–500 (varies)

Anganwadi Worker Salary / Honorarium 2026

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers receive a monthly honorarium (not a salary), which includes a Central Government component and an additional State Government component. The table below shows the total honorarium per month for key states in 2026.

State Anganwadi Worker Anganwadi Helper Supervisor
Central Govt (Base) ₹4,500/mo ₹2,250/mo -
Punjab ₹11,500/mo ₹7,000/mo ₹25,000/mo
Uttar Pradesh ₹10,000/mo ₹5,000/mo ₹20,000/mo
Maharashtra ₹12,000/mo ₹6,500/mo ₹28,000/mo
Karnataka ₹14,000/mo ₹7,000/mo ₹30,000/mo
Tamil Nadu ₹13,500/mo ₹6,500/mo ₹28,500/mo
Bihar ₹8,000/mo ₹4,500/mo ₹18,000/mo
Rajasthan ₹10,000/mo ₹5,500/mo ₹22,000/mo
Odisha ₹9,500/mo ₹5,000/mo ₹20,000/mo

Note: Honorarium figures are approximate and may vary. Refer to official state notification for exact figures. Additional benefits include ESI, provident fund (where applicable), and national health insurance.

Selection Process for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026

There is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Helper posts. Selection is merit-based on academic qualifications. For Supervisor and CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted.

How to Apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Online

  • Visit the official portal: Go to your state's official WCD / ICDS website or the direct link provided in the vacancy table above. For central information, visit wcd.nic.in.
  • Find the recruitment notification: Click on the "Recruitment" or "Anganwadi Recruitment 2026" section.
  • Download and read the notification: Download and read the official notification PDF carefully before applying.
  • Register / Login: Create a new account using your mobile number and Aadhaar. If already registered, log in with your credentials.
  • Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualification, address, and select your preferred district/ward. Ensure you apply only for a ward where you are a permanent resident.
  • Upload documents: Upload scanned copies of your passport-size photograph, signature, 10th/12th marksheet, Aadhaar card, and any caste/domicile certificate if required.
  • Review and submit: Check all entries carefully before final submission. Download and print the application confirmation / acknowledgement slip for your records.

Documents Required for Anganwadi Application

  • Aadhaar Card (Mandatory)
  • 10th Marksheet & Certificate
  • 12th Marksheet (for AWW)
  • Graduation Certificate (Supervisor)
  • Domicile / Residence Certificate
  • Caste Certificate (SC/ST/OBC)
  • Passport-size Photograph
  • Signature (scanned)
  • EWS Certificate (if applicable)
  • PwD Certificate (if applicable)

About this page: Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 information is compiled by the OnlyJobs editorial team directly from official state WCD / ICDS recruitment notifications. We verify all vacancy details, eligibility, and last dates before publishing. Page is updated daily as new notifications are released. For any discrepancy, always refer to the official notification PDF linked in the apply column above.

Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – FAQ's

How to apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 online?

Visit your state's official WCD / ICDS portal (link provided in table above). Register with mobile & Aadhaar, fill the application form, upload documents (photo, 10th certificate, Aadhaar, domicile), and submit before the last date. Print the application acknowledgement slip after submission.

What is the eligibility for Anganwadi Worker 2026?

Candidates must have passed 10th or 12th standard from a recognized board. Age limit is 18–35 years (varies by state). Must be a permanent resident of the ward/village for which vacancy exists. Only female candidates are eligible for Worker and Helper posts.

What is the salary of Anganwadi Worker in 2026?

Anganwadi Worker receives a monthly honorarium of ₹10,000–₹20,000 depending on the state (Karnataka and TN are highest at ~₹14,000). Anganwadi Helper gets ₹5,000–₹7,000/month. The Central Government pays ₹4,500/month as base honorarium, and states add their own component on top.

Is there a written exam for Anganwadi jobs?

No, there is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Anganwadi Helper posts. Selection is purely merit-based on academic qualification marks (10th / 12th percentage). For Supervisor / CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted depending on the state.

What is the age limit for Anganwadi Helper?

Age limit is 18 to 35 years as of 1st January 2026 in most states. SC/ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (maximum age 40). OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (maximum age 38). PwD candidates get additional relaxation as per state rules.

What documents are required for Anganwadi job application?

Required documents: Aadhaar card, 10th/12th marksheet and certificate, domicile/residence certificate, caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC), passport-size photograph, scanned signature, and EWS/PwD certificate if applicable. Graduation certificate is needed for Supervisor post applications.

What is ICDS recruitment 2026?

ICDS stands for Integrated Child Development Services - the government programme under which Anganwadi centres operate nationwide. ICDS recruitment 2026 refers to hiring of Anganwadi Workers, Helpers, Supervisors, and CDPOs by state WCD departments under the Ministry of Women & Child Development (wcd.nic.in).

Is Anganwadi Worker a permanent government job?

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers are honorary workers, not regular government employees. They receive a fixed monthly honorarium (not salary) and are entitled to benefits like ESI, health insurance, and maternity benefits. Supervisors and CDPOs are regular government employees in most states and receive full pay & grade pay.

Can I apply for Anganwadi job from a different district?

No. Anganwadi Workers and Helpers must be permanent residents of the same ward/village/district where the vacancy exists. A domicile or residence certificate is mandatory. Applications from outside the specified locality are rejected. For Supervisor/CDPO posts, the same district residency rule generally applies.

Forest Department Posts List

Forest departments work with a clear hierarchy from senior Indian Forest Service officers to field-level staff like Forest Guard and Forest Watcher. Understanding these posts helps candidates know growth opportunities and career progression in forest services.

  • Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (PCCF) / Head of Forest Force
  • Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (APCCF) / Additional Chief Conservator of Forests
  • Chief Conservator of Forests (CCF)
  • Conservator of Forests (CF)
  • Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF) / Divisional Forest Officer (DFO)
  • Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF)
  • Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Range Forest Officer (RFO)
  • Deputy Forest Ranger / Forest Inspector
  • Section Forest Officer / Forester / Van Daroga
  • Beat Forest Officer / Beat Guard / Forester Grade I
  • Forest Guard / Van Rakshak
  • Forest Watcher / Forest Worker / Plantation Worker
  • Forest Ranger (in states where used separately)
  • Field Guard

Most forest department recruitment notifications on this page focus on Forest Guard, Forester, Forest Range Officer, Field Guard and similar Group B, C and D posts.

Eligibility for Forest Department Jobs

Eligibility for forest department jobs depends on the post, state and recruitment authority. Below is a general idea of qualification and age for popular posts like Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer. Candidates should always check the official notification for detailed eligibility criteria.

Education Qualification for Forest Guard, Forester, Ranger

  • Forest Guard / Field Guard / Forest Watcher / Beat Guard: Usually 10th pass or 12th pass from a recognized board, knowledge of local language, and basic physical standards as per state rules.
  • Forester / Section Forest Officer / Deputy Forest Ranger: Generally 12th pass or graduate in any stream or science-related subjects, with physical standards and sometimes driving or technical skills depending on the post.
  • Forest Range Officer (RFO) / Forest Ranger / ACF: Normally graduate degree in Science, Agriculture, Forestry, Environmental Science, Engineering or related disciplines, with higher physical standards and sometimes a competitive exam similar to state PCS level.
  • IFS / Indian Forest Service and senior posts (DCF, CF, CCF, PCCF): Filled through UPSC Indian Forest Service Examination and promotions, requiring a graduate degree and all-India competitive selection.

Age Limit & Relaxation

  • Forest Guard / Forester / Field Guard: Minimum age is usually 18 years and maximum age is around 27–30 years, with age relaxation for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, Ex-servicemen and other categories as per government rules.
  • Forest Range Officer / ACF and higher competitive posts: Age limit often ranges from 21 to 32 years (varies by recruitment), with relaxations for reserved categories.
  • Exact age limit, cut-off date and relaxation details always depend on the particular notification, so candidates should verify from the official advertisement before applying.

Forest Department Jobs Salary Overview

Forest department salary depends on the post, pay level, state and allowances. Senior officers under Indian Forest Service receive higher pay levels, while Forest Guard, Forester and similar posts fall under state government pay matrices.

Post Pay Level Basic Pay Gross Salary (Approx) Category

Forester / Forest Sub-Inspector Level 6 ₹35,400 ₹45,000 – ₹56,000 Group C
Forest Beat Officer / Beat Guard / Junior Ranger Level 4 ₹25,500 ₹33,000 – ₹42,000 Group C

Selection Process & Exam Pattern

The selection process for Forest Department Jobs 2026 typically includes written examinations, physical tests and document verification. Some posts also conduct medical examination and walking tests due to the demanding nature of field duties.

Written Exam, PET/PST & Document Verification

  • Written Exam: Most Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer recruitments start with a written exam based on objective-type questions (MCQs).
  • PET / PST (Physical Efficiency Test / Physical Standard Test): Candidates who qualify written exam are called for running, walking test, height, chest measurement and other physical parameters as per post and gender.
  • Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates must produce original certificates of age, qualification, caste, residence, sports/NCC and other documents mentioned in the notification.
  • Medical Examination: Many forest posts require medical fitness to ensure candidates can perform field duties in forests, wildlife areas and difficult terrain.

Syllabus Highlights for Forest Guard & Ranger

Typical syllabus for written exam may include:

  • General Knowledge & Current Affairs (national, state-level, environment and forest-related topics)
  • General Hindi / English or regional language (grammar, comprehension, vocabulary)
  • General Mathematics / Quantitative Aptitude (up to 10th or 12th level)
  • General Science and Environmental Studies (forest, wildlife, biodiversity, conservation, climate, basic biology)
  • Reasoning Ability (logical and analytical reasoning)

Exact subject-wise marks, number of questions, negative marking and exam duration will be given in the respective forest department notification.

How to Apply for Forest Department Jobs 2026

Most Forest Department Jobs 2026 applications are accepted either online through official websites or offline via prescribed application forms. Candidates should carefully read the official notification before filling the form.

Online Application Process

  1. Visit the official website of the recruiting body (State Forest Department, State PSC, Staff Selection Board or other government portal).
  2. Find the latest link for Forest Guard / Forester / Forest Range Officer / other forest posts and open the detailed advertisement.
  3. Complete the registration process with basic details like name, mobile number, email ID and verify using OTP if required.
  4. Fill the online application form with personal, educational, address and category information exactly as per documents.
  5. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature and other certificates in the prescribed format and size.
  6. Pay the application fee online through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card or UPI as per your convenience.
  7. Finally, submit the form and take a printout or PDF of the submitted application and payment receipt for future reference.

Documents Required & Application Fee

  • Recent passport size photograph and signature (scanned)
  • Class 10th and 12th mark sheets and certificates
  • Degree / Diploma certificates for graduate-level posts
  • Caste / Category certificate (SC, ST, OBC, EWS, etc.)
  • Domicile / Residence certificate if required by the state
  • NCC, Sports, Ex-serviceman and other quota certificates if applicable
  • Valid ID proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN, Voter ID, Driving Licence, Passport, etc.)

Why Choose Forest Department Jobs?

Forest department jobs are popular among candidates who love nature, wildlife and field work. These jobs offer job security, government benefits and a chance to work in forest conservation, wildlife protection, eco-tourism and community development.

  • Stable government job with regular salary, pension/NPS and allowances
  • Opportunity to work in forests, wildlife sanctuaries, national parks and protected areas
  • Contribution to environment protection, afforestation and biodiversity conservation
  • Good promotion avenues from Forest Guard to Forester, Forest Range Officer and higher posts in many states.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Power Grid Corporation of India Limited (POWERGRID) has published the Diploma Trainee, Junior Officer Trainee & Junior Technician Trainee Notification 2026 in April 2026. The recruitment drive aims to fill 660 positions (630 for POWERGRID + 30 for CTUIL). Candidates within the age range of 18 to 27 years and holding a Diploma in Engineering / Graduate Degree (BBA/BBM/BBS) / Inter CA/CMA / ITI in relevant disciplines are eligible to apply online at powergrid.in. The application process will be active from 20th April 2026 to 11th May 2026, and candidates can easily access the notification PDF and apply link below.

POWERGRID Diploma Trainee, Junior Officer Trainee & Junior Technician Trainee Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Power Grid Corporation of India (POWERGRID), a ‘Maharatna’ Public Sector Enterprise under the Ministry of Power, Govt. of India has released the detailed advertisement (CC/01/2026) for Diploma Trainee, Junior Officer Trainee and Junior Technician Trainee posts (including vacancies for its subsidiary CTUIL). Candidates can download the official detailed advertisement from the POWERGRID website and check region-wise vacancies, eligibility, fee and application process before applying online.

  • Download POWERGRID Recruitment 2026 Detailed Advertisement PDF (Advt. No. CC/01/2026)

POWERGRID Diploma Trainee, Junior Officer Trainee & Junior Technician Trainee Recruitment 2026 Overview

Particulars Details
Organisation POWERGRID Corporation of India Limited (Maharatna PSU) & CTUIL (wholly owned subsidiary)
Recruitment Name Non-Executive Posts Recruitment 2026
Posts Diploma Trainee (Electrical) - DTE
Diploma Trainee (Civil) - DTC
Diploma Trainee (Survey) - DTS
Junior Officer Trainee (HR) - JOT (HR)
Junior Officer Trainee (F&A) - JOT (F&A)
Junior Technician Trainee (Survey) - JTT (Survey)
Total Vacancies POWERGRID: 630 + 8* (backlog)
CTUIL: 30
(Region / Project specific - not interlinked)
Training Duration One Year
Age Limit 27 years as on 11.05.2026
Application Mode Online only
Official Website www.powergrid.in

POWERGRID Diploma Trainee, Junior Officer Trainee & Junior Technician Trainee Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Notification Date 17 April 2026
Online Application Start Date 20 April 2026 (17:00 Hours)
Last Date for Online Application 11 May 2026 (23:59 Hours)
Last Date for Payment of Fee 11 May 2026 (23:59 Hours)

POWERGRID Diploma Trainee, Junior Officer Trainee & Junior Technician Trainee Vacancy 2026

A total of 660 vacancies have been announced (630 for POWERGRID + 30 for CTUIL) on region-wise basis across various Regions/Projects of POWERGRID and CTUIL.

POWERGRID Vacancy (630 Posts)

Region/Project Total Posts
Corporate Centre 21
Eastern Region-I (ER-I) 2
Eastern Region-II (ER-II) 16
Odisha Project 32
North Eastern Region (NER) 33
Northern Region-I (NR-I) 92
Rajasthan Project 42
Northern Region-II (NR-II) 50
Northern Region-III (NR-III) 45
Southern Region-I (SR-I) 47
Southern Region-II (SR-II) 81
Western Region-I (WR-I) 67
Western Region-II (WR-II) 110

CTUIL Vacancy (30 Posts)

Post Total Posts
Diploma Trainee (Electrical) - DTE 16
Diploma Trainee (Civil) - DTC 6
Junior Officer Trainee (HR) - JOT (HR) 4
Junior Officer Trainee (F&A) - JOT (F&A) 4

POWERGRID Diploma Trainee, Junior Officer Trainee & Junior Technician Trainee Eligibility Criteria 2026

  • Nationality: Only Indian Nationals of age 18 years or above are eligible to apply.

Diploma Trainee Posts (DTE / DTC / DTS)

Post Name Educational Qualification
Diploma Trainee (Electrical) - DTE Full Time Regular Three Years Diploma in relevant discipline of engineering - Electrical/ Electrical (Power)/ Electrical and Electronics/ Power Systems Engineering/ Power Engineering (Electrical), from recognized Technical Board/Institute with minimum 70% marks for General/ OBC (NCL)/ EWS Candidates and pass marks for SC/ST/PwBD. Higher technical qualification like B.Tech/BE/M.Tech/ME etc. with or without Diploma is not allowed.
Diploma Trainee (Civil) - DTC Full Time Regular Three Years Diploma in Civil Engineering from recognized Technical Board/Institute with minimum 70% marks for General/ OBC (NCL)/ EWS Candidates and pass marks for SC/ST/PwBD. Higher technical qualification like B.Tech/BE/M.Tech/ME etc. with or without Diploma is not allowed.
Diploma Trainee (Survey) - DTS Full-Time Regular Three Years Diploma in Survey Engineering OR Diploma in Civil Engineering (with survey as a subject) from recognized Technical Board / Institute having minimum 70% marks. Higher technical qualification like B.E./B.Tech etc. with or without Diploma is not allowed.

Junior Officer Trainee & Junior Technician Trainee Posts

Post Name Educational Qualification
Junior Officer Trainee (HR) - JOT (HR) Three years full time Graduate Regular Degree - BBA/ BBM/ BBS or equivalent qualification from recognized Institute/ University with not less than 60% marks for General/ EWS/ OBC (NCL) category candidates. Candidates with Post Graduate Degree/Diploma or equivalent higher education qualification shall not be allowed to apply.
Junior Officer Trainee (F&A) - JOT (F&A) Inter CA/ Inter CMA. Candidates with Post Graduate Degree/ Post Graduate Diploma/ CA/ CMA or equivalent higher education qualification shall not be allowed to apply.
Junior Technician Trainee (Survey) - JTT (Survey) Full-Time Regular Two Years ITI (Surveyor) from a recognized Technical Board/ Institute. Higher technical qualification like Diploma/B.E./B.Tech etc. with or without ITI is not allowed.

POWERGRID Diploma Trainee, Junior Officer Trainee & Junior Technician Trainee Age Limit 2026

  • All Posts - 27 Years (Upper Age Limit as on 11-05-2026)
Category Age Limit
Minimum Age 18 years
Maximum Age (General) 27 years
OBC (NCL) 3 years relaxation
SC / ST 5 years relaxation
PwBD (UR/EWS) 10 years relaxation
PwBD (OBC-NCL) 13 years relaxation
PwBD (SC/ST) 15 years relaxation
Ex-Servicemen As per Govt. of India directives (period of service + 3 years, subject to conditions)

POWERGRID Diploma Trainee, Junior Officer Trainee & Junior Technician Trainee Application Fee 2026

  • For General / OBC (NCL) / EWS: ₹300/- (for DTE/DTC/DTS/JOT posts) or ₹200/- (for JTT Survey post)
  • For  SC / ST / PwBD / Ex-Servicemen: No fee (exempted)

POWERGRID Diploma Trainee, Junior Officer Trainee & Junior Technician Trainee Selection Process 2026

  • Written Test / Computer Based Test (CBT) - 100% weightage (170 objective questions: 120 Technical/Professional Knowledge + 50 Aptitude)
  • Computer Skill Test (CST) (for JOT HR / F&A) - Qualifying in nature
  • Trade Test (TT) (for JTT Survey) - Qualifying in nature

Pre-Employment Medical Examination

  • CBT Qualifying Criteria: Minimum 40% aggregate (UR/EWS) / 30% aggregate (reserved) with separate minimum in each part (for Diploma / JOT posts). For JTT: 40% (UR/EWS) / 30% (reserved).

    Final merit is based on CBT marks only (CST / Trade Test is only qualifying).

POWERGRID Non-Executive Recruitment 2026 Documents Required

Candidates must upload the following documents while applying online (and produce originals at Document Verification):

  • Recent passport size colour photograph & signature
  • Date of Birth proof (Matric / Birth Certificate)
  • Qualification Certificates & all Mark Sheets (with CGPA to % conversion proof if applicable)
  • No Objection Certificate (for Govt./PSU employees)
  • Caste / EWS / PwBD Certificate (if applicable) in GOI format
  • Scribe-related certificates (if applicable)
  • Ex-Servicemen certificates / Discharge Certificate (if applicable)
  • Domicile cum Age relaxation certificate for Riot Victims (if applicable)

How to Apply for POWERGRID Recruitment 2026

  • Candidates must apply online only through https://www.powergrid.in → Career Section.
  • Upload recent passport size photograph (50 KB), signature (50 KB) and all required documents in prescribed format.
  • Pay application fee online (if applicable) through Payment Gateway.
  • Candidates can edit/update application till the last date. No changes allowed after closing.
  • Take printout of the submitted online application for future reference.
  • Last date for submission of online application is 11 May 2026 (23:59 Hours).

POWERGRID Diploma Trainee, Junior Officer Trainee & Junior Technician Trainee Online Application Form

Eligible candidates who wish to apply for the POWERGRID Diploma Trainee, Junior Officer Trainee and Junior Technician Trainee posts can do so by submitting their application form online through the official POWERGRID website. The application process for the POWERGRID Recruitment 2026 is completely online, and candidates must ensure that they complete all steps before the deadline.

The online application process for the POWERGRID Non-Executive Recruitment 2026 will begin on 20 April 2026 at 5:00 PM through the POWERGRID career portal. Eligible candidates are encouraged to fill out the application form and upload the required documents including recent passport-sized photograph and signature in prescribed format.

Click Here to Apply Online for POWERGRID Non Executive Recruitment 2026

POWERGRID Diploma Trainee, Junior Officer Trainee & Junior Technician Trainee Remuneration 2026

Selected candidates shall undergo training for a period of one year. Pay during training & on successful completion of training is as given below.

Pay Component Details
Diploma Trainee / Junior Officer Trainee (during training - 1 year) Pay scale Rs.24000-3%-108000/- (IDA) at S0 level with Basic Pay Rs.24,000/- + IDA, HRA and Perks @12% of Basic Pay
On absorption (after training) Junior Engineer Gr-IV / Junior Officer Gr-IV at S1 level in pay scale Rs.25000-3%-117500/- (IDA) with annual CTC approx. Rs.12.7 Lakhs
Junior Technician Trainee (Survey) (during training - 1 year) Pay scale Rs.21000-3%-72000/- (IDA) at W2 level with Basic Pay Rs.21,000/- + IDA, HRA and Perks @12% of Basic Pay
On absorption (after training) Junior Technician at W3 level in pay scale Rs.21500-3%-74000/- (IDA) with annual CTC approx. Rs.10.9 Lakhs
Additional Benefits (on regularization) Dearness Allowance, Perquisites & Allowance as per cafeteria approach, Performance Related Pay, Company Quarters or HRA, Reimbursement of conveyance, mobile, laptop, PF, Gratuity, Pension, Leave encashment, Group Insurance, Medical facilities, Loans & Advances etc. as per company rules

Important Instructions for Candidates

  • Read the detailed advertisement carefully before filling the online application.
  • Upload legible copies of all required documents (photograph, signature, qualification certificates, caste/PwBD certificate etc.) in the prescribed size and format.
  • Candidates working in Govt./PSU must upload NOC or undertaking at the time of application.
  • Keep registered mobile number and email active till completion of the recruitment process.
  • Application fee once paid will not be refunded.
  • POWERGRID reserves the right to cancel or modify the recruitment process without assigning any reason.
  • Region-specific vacancies; no inter-region transfer request allowed after joining.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The LIC Housing Finance Limited (LIC HFL) has officially announced the Junior Assistant Recruitment 2026 with 180 vacancies across multiple states and union territories in India. The detailed notification was published on the official website lichousing.com. Candidates who meet the age criteria of 21 to 30 years and hold a graduate degree with minimum 55% aggregate marks from a UGC-recognized university along with computer knowledge can submit their online applications from 16 April 2026 to 30 April 2026. Direct links to download the official notification PDF and apply online are shared below.

LIC HFL Junior Assistant Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

LIC Housing Finance Limited (LIC HFL), one of India's largest and most trusted housing finance companies operating under the Life Insurance Corporation of India umbrella, has officially released the recruitment notification for the post of Junior Assistant for the Academic Year 2026-2027. A total of 180 vacancies have been announced across 20 states and union territories spanning major cities pan-India.

This recruitment is city-wise and merit-based, meaning candidates must apply for a specific Vacancy City and will be posted preferably in that city subject to requirement and suitability. The selection process involves an Online Examination followed by a Personal Interview. All applications must be submitted exclusively online through the official LIC HFL website. Candidates are strongly advised to read the complete official notification before applying to ensure full awareness of eligibility conditions, examination pattern, and application procedures.

  • Download LIC HFL Junior Assistant Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF
  • LIC HFL Junior Assistant Syllabus 2026

LIC HFL Junior Assistant Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name LIC Housing Finance Limited (LIC HFL)
Post Name Junior Assistant

Employment Type Regular / Permanent

Official Website www.lichousing.com

LIC HFL Junior Assistant Vacancy 2026

A total of 180 vacancies have been announced across 20 states and union territories. Karnataka has the highest number of vacancies at 29 (of which 23 are in Bangalore alone), followed by Maharashtra (25), Telangana (24), Tamil Nadu (21), Madhya Pradesh (18), and Uttar Pradesh (13).

Candidates must note that the recruitment is city-specific - they can apply for only one Vacancy City. The actual posting of selected candidates will be as per merit drawn for each city. While candidates will be posted preferably in their chosen Vacancy City, this is subject to requirement and suitability and shall not be deemed implicit. The total vacancy count may increase or decrease at the time of final selection depending on actual availability.

LIC HFL Junior Assistant Eligibility Criteria 2026

Criteria Details
Nationality Indian
Age (as on 01.04.2026) 21 to 30 years (Born between 02/04/1996 and 01/04/2005, both inclusive)
Educational Qualification Graduate with minimum 55% aggregate marks in any discipline from a UGC-recognized University
Mode of Study Regular mode only (Correspondence / Distance / Part-time not eligible)
Computer Literacy Mandatory - operating and working knowledge of computer systems
Work Experience Preferred
Result Declaration Must be declared on or before 01/04/2026

LIC HFL Junior Assistant Remuneration & Benefits 2026

Selected Junior Assistants will receive a competitive CTC package that varies based on the city category (Category I, II, or III) in which they are posted.

The basic pay is uniform at ₹20,000 across all city categories, but HRA and other benefits differ based on city classification. In addition to salary, Junior Assistants are eligible for Gratuity, Staff Housing Loan Scheme, and Performance Linked Incentive as per applicable company rules. Annual increments of up to 10% of Basic Pay are provided based on quarterly/annual performance.

The company's Meal Coupon is payable at ₹210 per working day (for approximately 24 working days per month), though this may vary month to month. It is important to note that Lease Facility and Staff Quarter Facility are not applicable for the Junior Assistant cadre. The probation period is 6 months from the date of joining, extendable as per company rules.

CTC Structure (Monthly & Annual):

City Category Basic Pay Total CTC (Monthly) Total CTC (Annual)
I 20,000 ₹41,509 ₹4,98,108
II 20,000 ₹39,909 ₹4,78,908
III 20,000 ₹38,709 ₹4,64,508

Net Pay Structure (After Deductions, Monthly):

City Category Gross Pay Total Deductions Net Pay (Monthly) Meal Coupon
I ₹32,800 ₹3,273 ₹29,527 ₹5,040
II ₹31,200 ₹3,273 ₹27,927 ₹5,040
III ₹30,000 ₹3,273 ₹26,727 ₹5,040

LIC HFL Junior Assistant Selection Process 2026

The selection process for LIC HFL Junior Assistant consists of three stages: an Online Examination, a Personal Interview, and a Medical & Background Verification check. The Online Examination is objective type, multiple choice, conducted in English only, with a total duration of 2 hours and 200 questions carrying 200 marks across 5 sections. There is negative marking of 0.25 marks for every wrong answer. No penalty is applied for unattempted questions. The scores are equated across sessions to account for any minor variation in difficulty level.

Only candidates who rank sufficiently high in the online examination will be called for interview. Candidates who fail to secure minimum qualifying marks in the interview will be disqualified. The final selection is based on the combined marks of the Online Examination and Interview, strictly in order of merit. Medical examination and background verification are the final mandatory steps before appointment, carried out at the candidate's own expense.

Online Examination Pattern:

Sr. No. Section No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration
1 English Language 40 40 120 minutes (combined)
2 Logical Reasoning 40 40  
3 General Awareness (with special emphasis on Housing Finance Industry) 40 40  
4 Numerical Ability 40 40  
5 Computer Skill 40 40  
Total   200 200 120 minutes

Selection Stages:

Stage Details
1. Online Examination Objective type, 200 questions, 200 marks, 2 hours, English only, negative marking (0.25 per wrong answer)
2. Personal Interview Only high-merit candidates from online exam are called; minimum qualifying marks required
3. Final Merit List Based on combined marks of Online Examination + Interview
4. Medical Examination Conducted by LIC HFL authorized Medical Examiner; at candidate's own expense
5. Background Verification Carried out post-selection; adverse findings may lead to termination

LIC HFL Junior Assistant Application Fee 2026

The application fee is applicable to all candidates regardless of category, and is non-refundable under all circumstances. Payment must be made online only through the available digital payment modes. Bank transaction charges for online payment will be borne by the candidate. GST at 18% will be charged on the application fee in addition to the base fee amount. Candidates are advised to pay the fees and complete registration early to avoid last-minute payment gateway issues or internet congestion. Once the fee is paid and application is submitted, no refund will be made even in the case of ineligibility or withdrawal.

Post Application Fee
Junior Assistant ₹800/- + 18% GST

Payment Modes Accepted: Debit Cards (RuPay/Visa/MasterCard/Maestro), Credit Cards, Internet Banking, IMPS, Cash Cards, Mobile Wallets.

How to Apply for LIC HFL Junior Assistant Recruitment 2026

The application process is entirely online through the official LIC HFL website. Before registering, candidates must scan and keep ready their recent passport-size photograph (4.5cm × 3.5cm), signature (black ink on white paper), left thumb impression (black or blue ink on white paper), and a handwritten declaration in English on white paper with black ink. The handwritten declaration text must be: "I, _______ (Name of the candidate), hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required.

" This declaration must be written by the candidate themselves in English - declarations written by others or in any other language will render the application invalid. Signature in CAPITAL LETTERS is not accepted. Candidates must ensure a valid personal email ID and active mobile number are available throughout the recruitment process, as all communications will be sent only through these channels. Once the application is submitted and confirmed, no changes will be permitted under any circumstances.

Step-by-Step Application Process:

  1. Visit www.lichousing.com → Click on "Careers" → "Job Opportunities" → "Recruitment of Junior Assistants" → "Apply Online"
  2. Click "Click here for New Registration" → Enter Name, Contact Details, Email ID → Note Provisional Registration Number and Password
  3. Fill the application form; use "SAVE AND NEXT" to save progress at each step
  4. Upload Photograph, Signature, Left Thumb Impression, and Handwritten Declaration as per specifications
  5. Preview the entire application carefully before final submission
  6. Click "COMPLETE REGISTRATION" only after verifying all details
  7. Proceed to "Payment" Tab → Pay application fee online → Download e-Receipt
  8. Take a printout of the system-generated application form on A-4 size paper and retain it

Document Upload Specifications:

Document Format Size Dimensions
Photograph JPG/JPEG 20 KB – 50 KB 200 × 230 pixels (preferred)
Signature JPG/JPEG 10 KB – 20 KB 140 × 60 pixels (preferred)
Left Thumb Impression JPG/JPEG 20 KB – 50 KB 240 × 240 pixels / 200 DPI
Handwritten Declaration JPG/JPEG 50 KB – 100 KB 800 × 400 pixels / 200 DPI

Apply Online at: www.lichousing.com

Important Instructions for Candidates

Candidates must ensure all information filled in the online application is accurate and complete before final submission, as no changes are allowed after clicking "COMPLETE REGISTRATION." Only one application per candidate is permitted - in case of multiple registrations, only the one with the highest registration number will be retained and fees paid for other registrations will be forfeited. In-service candidates must produce a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their current employer at the time of interview.

Candidates appearing for the online examination must bring the Call Letter along with original photo identity proof and a photocopy of the same. Mobile phones, pagers, calculators, and all electronic communication devices are strictly prohibited inside the examination hall. Candidates are advised to report to the examination centre well before the reporting time specified in the call letter, as late arrivals will not be permitted to take the examination.

Biometric data (thumb impression and photograph) will be captured on the day of the online examination and verified subsequently. Any candidate found guilty of using unfair means, impersonation, or misconduct will be disqualified and may be debarred from future LIC HFL recruitment processes permanently.

  • Read the complete notification carefully before applying.
  • Apply online only at www.lichousing.com under the "Careers" section.
  • Online applications with incomplete details, incorrect photo/signature uploads, or unsuccessful fee payment will be rejected.
  • Canvassing in any form will lead to immediate disqualification.
  • All legal disputes will fall under the jurisdiction of Mumbai courts only.
  • LIC HFL reserves the right to alter, modify, cancel, or change any part of the recruitment process at any stage without prior notice.

SSC CHSL Tier 2 Answer Key 2026 – Download PDF, Response Sheet & Objection Link

The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has released the SSC CHSL Tier 2 Tentative Answer Key 2026. Candidates who appeared for the SSC CHSL Tier 2 examination held on 10th April 2026 can now check and download the tentative answer key along with response sheet from the official website ssc.gov.in.

This recruitment drive aims to fill 3131 vacancies for LDC, JSA, PA, SA, and DEO posts. If you find any discrepancy in the answers, you can raise objections before 23rd April 2026 (06:00 PM).

SSC CHSL Tier 2 Answer Key 2026 – Quick Overview

SSC CHSL Tier 2 Answer Key 2026
www.OnlyJobs
Organization Staff Selection Commission (SSC)
Post Name Combined Higher Secondary Level (CHSL) Tier 2
Total Vacancies 3131
Exam Date 10th April 2026
Answer Key Status Released (Tentative)
Release Date 20th April 2026
Last Date for Objection 23rd April 2026 (06:00 PM)
Official Website ssc.gov.in

Direct Link: Download SSC CHSL Tier 2 Answer Key 2026 PDF (Tentative)

How to Download SSC CHSL Tier 2 Answer Key 2026 ?

Follow these simple steps to download your answer key:

  1. Visit the official website: ssc.gov.in
  2. Click on “Login/Register” and log in to the candidate’s dashboard
  3. Select SSC CHSL 2025 (Tier-II) examination
  4. Click on the link for “Uploading of Tentative Answer Key(s) along with Candidates’ Response Sheet(s)”
  5. Log in using your Registration Number and Password
  6. Your answer key with recorded responses will appear on screen
  7. Download and save the PDF for future reference

SSC CHSL Tier 2 Answer Key 2026 – Important Links

Download Answer Key 2026 & Response Sheet Click Here (Tentative)
Official Website ssc.gov.in
Download Mobile App
Join Telegram Channel
Join WhatsApp Channel

How to Raise Objection Against SSC CHSL Tier 2 Answer Key 2026 ?

If you disagree with any answer in the tentative key, follow these steps:

  1. Log in to your candidate portal on ssc.gov.in
  2. Go to the “Challenge/Objection” section (Challenge Management Portal)
  3. Select the question(s) you want to challenge
  4. Provide reason/explanation and upload supporting documents (if required)
  5. Pay the objection fee (₹50 per question, non-refundable)
  6. Submit before the deadline: 23rd April 2026 (06:00 PM)

Important: Objections submitted after the deadline will NOT be considered. Objection window is open from 20th April 2026 (01:00 PM) to 23rd April 2026 (06:00 PM).

SSC CHSL Tier 2 Answer Key 2026 – Important Dates

Event Date
Exam Conducted 10th April 2026
Tentative Answer Key Released 20th April 2026
Last Date to Raise Objection 23rd April 2026 (06:00 PM)
Final Answer Key (Expected) May 2026 (after objection review)
Result Declaration (Expected) To be announced (First Round of Tentative Allocation expected in May 2026)

How to Calculate Your Score Using SSC CHSL Tier 2 Answer Key 2026 ?

Use this formula to estimate your score:

Total Score = (Correct Answers × Marks per Question) – (Wrong Answers × Negative Marking)

Marking Scheme for SSC CHSL Tier 2 Exam:

  • Correct Answer: +2 or +3 marks (as per section)
  • Wrong Answer: -0.50 or -1 mark (negative marking applicable in certain sections)
  • Unattempted: 0 marks

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The Jammu & Kashmir Bank Limited (J&K Bank) has officially announced the Apprentice Recruitment 2026 under the NATS (National Apprenticeship Training Scheme) with 614 vacancies for a training period of one year. The detailed notification (Ref. No. JKB/HR/Rectt/2026-182) was released on 17 April 2026 on the official website jkb.bank.in. Candidates who meet the age criteria of 20 to 28 years and possess a graduation degree (passed on or after 01 January 2022) can submit their online applications from 20 April 2026 to 26 April 2026. Direct links to download the official notification PDF and apply online are provided below.

J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organisation Name Jammu & Kashmir Bank Limited
Reference No. JKB/HR/Rectt/2026-182
Post Name Apprentice (under NATS -National Apprenticeship Training Scheme)
Total Vacancies 614 Posts
Training Duration 12 Months
Qualification Graduation from a recognised University/Institute (passed on or after 01.01.2022)
Application Mode Online Only
Opening Date for Online Registration 20-04-2026

J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria

Eligibility Criterion Description
Qualification Graduation from a recognised University/Institute. Must have passed graduation on or after 01.01.2022. Proficiency in local/official language required (studied as subject in Class 10 or 12).
Age (as on 01/01/2026) Minimum 20 years and Maximum 28 years. Relaxation for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD etc. as per Government guidelines.
Training Duration 12 months. Apprentices will be relieved after completion of the apprenticeship period.
Stipend ₹13,500/- per month (Bank's share: ₹9,000/- + Govt. share via DBT: ₹4,500/-). Conveyance up to ₹1,250/- per month. DBT amount applicable only for candidates below 35 years of age.
Trainee Hours On-the-Job Training during normal working hours from 10 AM to 5 PM, as applicable to the workmen cadre of the Bank.

J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay a non-refundable application fee/intimation charges through online mode only during the registration window from 20 April 2026 to 26 April 2026. No other mode of payment is available. The application fee is inclusive of GST, and bank transaction charges for online payment will have to be borne by the candidate.

Fee once remitted will not be refunded under any circumstances, including withdrawal of candidature or cancellation of the recruitment process.Candidates should ensure they pay the fee applicable to their correct category, as change of category will not be permitted at any stage after submission of the online application. Payment can be made using Debit Cards (RuPay/Visa/MasterCard/Maestro), Credit Cards, Internet Banking, IMPS, Cash Cards, or Mobile Wallets.

Category Application Fee
Unreserved (General) Category ₹700/- (inclusive of GST)
Reserved Category Candidates ₹500/- (inclusive of GST)

J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Selection Process

The selection of apprentices for J&K Bank 2026 will be based entirely on merit obtained in an Online Written Examination of objective type. The examination consists of 100 questions of 100 marks to be completed in 60 minutes (1 hour), divided into two sections: General Awareness & English Comprehension (50 questions, 50 marks, 30 minutes) and Quantitative Aptitude & Reasoning Ability (50 questions, 50 marks, 30 minutes).

There is a penalty for wrong answers -one-fourth (1/4th) of the marks assigned to a question will be deducted for each wrong answer. No penalty applies for unattempted questions. The online examination will be conducted only in English. The merit list will be drawn district/region and category wise, with candidates placed according to their aggregate marks in descending order. In case two or more candidates score equal marks, they will be ranked based on age in descending order (older candidate ranked higher).

A wait list of 50% of notified vacancies will be maintained for one year from the date of result or till the next engagement exercise, whichever is earlier. The Bank reserves the right to conduct any other test or follow any other selection criteria at its discretion.

S. No. Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration
1 General Awareness & English Comprehension 50 50 30 Minutes
2 Quantitative Aptitude & Reasoning Ability 50 50 30 Minutes
Total 100 100 60 Minutes

Additional Benefits for J&K / Ladakh Domicile Apprentices (on Future Recruitment):

  • Relaxation of one year in age for appointment in Banking Associate cadre after successful completion of the Apprenticeship programme (applicable exclusively for domicile of UTs of J&K and Ladakh).
  • An additional 2.5% of maximum marks (i.e., 2.5 marks out of 100) will be added to the score in the online written test, subject to the condition that total marks (including bonus marks) do not exceed maximum marks (applicable exclusively for domicile of UTs of J&K and Ladakh).

J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Application Form

The application form for J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is available exclusively through the online mode on the official J&K Bank website at https://www.jkb.bank.in. No offline or paper-based application form will be accepted under any circumstances. Before filling the online application form, candidates must first complete their registration on the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS) portal at https://nats.education.gov.in and obtain their enrolment number. Candidates must ensure that their Mobile number, Aadhaar, and Bank account are inter-linked and DBT is enabled in their bank account before proceeding with the application.

The online application form requires candidates to fill in personal details, educational qualification details, category details, district/area preference, and upload scanned documents including photograph, signature, left thumb impression, and a handwritten declaration in English. Candidates are advised to verify all details carefully before clicking the final "Complete Registration" button, as no change will be permitted after final submission. After successful payment of the application fee, candidates must take a printout of the e-Receipt and the online application form for future reference.

Eligible candidates wishing to apply for J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 must complete the online application process between 20 April 2026 and 26 April 2026. Applications submitted after the closing date or through any mode other than online will not be considered.

Submit Online Application at: https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/jkbamar26/ 

J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Salary / Stipend

Component Amount Disbursed By
Bank's Share of Stipend ₹9,000/- per month J&K Bank (directly)
Government's Share of Stipend ₹4,500/- per month Government of India via DBT (for candidates below 35 years of age and first-time apprentices only)
Total Monthly Stipend ₹13,500/- per month Combined (Bank + Govt.)
Conveyance Allowance Up to ₹1,250/- per month J&K Bank
Other Allowances / Benefits NIL Not Applicable

How to Apply for J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026

  • Eligible candidates can apply for J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 through online mode only from 20 April 2026 to 26 April 2026. No other mode of application will be accepted.
  • Before applying, candidates must first register on the Apprenticeship Portal at https://nats.education.gov.in and obtain their enrolment number.
  • Candidates must complete 100% of their profile on the Apprenticeship Portal and ensure their Mobile number, Aadhaar, and Bank account are inter-linked with DBT enabled.
  • Thereafter, candidates must apply on the official J&K Bank website at https://www.jkb.bank.in under the Careers tab by clicking on the "Engagement of Apprentices" link.
  • Candidates can apply for only one District/Area; if more than one application is submitted, only the latest application will be considered. Candidates must upload scanned copies of their photograph, signature, left thumb impression, and a handwritten declaration in English as per the specified format and dimensions.
  • After submitting the application and paying the fee, candidates must take a printout of the e-Receipt and online Application Form for future reference. No change of data is permitted after the final submission of the online application.

Documents to Upload at the Time of Online Application:

  • Passport-size colour photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm; 20KB–50KB; JPG/JPEG)
  • Signature in black ink on white paper (10KB–20KB; JPG/JPEG)
  • Left thumb impression on white paper with black or blue ink (20KB–50KB; JPG/JPEG)
  • Handwritten declaration in English on white paper with black ink (50KB–100KB; JPG/JPEG)

J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Examination Centres

The online written examination for J&K Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 will be conducted at designated centres across multiple cities.

The examination will be held online at venues mentioned in the respective call letters. No request for change of centre, venue, date, or session will be entertained under any circumstances. The choice of centre once exercised by the candidate is final.

The Bank reserves the right to cancel any examination centre, add new centres, or allot a different centre to a candidate depending on the response and administrative feasibility.

Candidates will appear for the examination at their own risk and expense. Though the duration of the examination is 1 hour, candidates may be required to remain at the venue for approximately 4 hours to complete formalities such as document verification and login procedures.

Candidates reporting late after the reporting time specified on the call letter will not be permitted to take the examination.

Available Examination Centres
Srinagar, J&K Bangalore, Karnataka Pune, Maharashtra
Jammu, J&K Baramulla, J&K Samba, J&K
Leh, Ladakh Kargil, Ladakh Delhi / Delhi NCR
Mumbai / Navi Mumbai / Thane / MMR, Maharashtra Lucknow, U.P. Chandigarh
Mohali    

Documents Required at the Time of Engagement

Candidates who are shortlisted and called for engagement as apprentices must produce the following documents in original along with a self-attested photocopy at the time of engagement. Non-submission of any of the required documents will make the candidate ineligible for further participation in the engagement process.

Candidates are advised to keep all documents ready well in advance to avoid last-minute delays. All documents must match exactly the details submitted in the online application form. Any discrepancy found in the documents at any stage of the process may lead to disqualification or termination of apprenticeship, if already engaged.

  • Valid system generated printout of online application
  • Proof of Date of Birth (SSLC / Class X Certificate with DOB)
  • Photocopy of photo identity proof bearing the same name as in the online application
  • Photocopy of Aadhaar and PAN Card
  • Mark sheets and certificates of Graduation or equivalent qualification
  • Two recent passport-size photographs
  • Printout of Online Result
  • Permanent Resident / Domicile Certificate
  • Other qualification certificates (if any)
  • Income and Asset Certificate issued by competent authority (for EWS candidates)
  • Category Certificate (if applicable)
  • Caste Certificate issued by competent authority in Government of India prescribed format (for SC/ST/OBC candidates); OBC certificate must contain non-creamy layer clause and must be valid as on the date of notification
  • PCP/Disability Certificate issued by the District Medical Board (for PwBD candidates)
  • Scribe certificate, if used during examination

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released the official Junior Engineer (Civil/ Electrical) Notification 2026 on 16 April 2026 at www.rbi.org.in. A total of 11 vacancies have been announced under the RBI Junior Engineer Recruitment 2026. Eligible candidates aged between 20 to 30 years with a qualification of Diploma/Degree in Civil or Electrical Engineering with required experience can apply online from 16-04-2026 to 06-05-2026. The official notification PDF and online application link are available below.

RBI Junior Engineer (Civil/ Electrical) Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Notification for Recruitment for the Post of Junior Engineer (Civil/ Electrical) in Reserve Bank of India - Panel Year 2026 is published on the official website rbi.org.in. The full text of the advertisement is available on the Bank's website (www.rbi.org.in) and is also being published in the Employment News/ Rojgar Samachar. Candidates should thoroughly read the official advertisement to check zone-wise vacancies, eligibility criteria, age limit, reservation details, selection process and detailed instructions for filling the application form.

Document Download Link
RBI Junior Engineer (Civil/ Electrical) Recruitment 2026 Detailed Advertisement Download PDF

RBI Junior Engineer (Civil/ Electrical) Recruitment 2026 - Overview

Particulars Details

RBI Junior Engineer Recruitment 2026 Zone-wise & Category-wise Vacancy Details

A total of 11 vacancies will be filled for Junior Engineer posts in RBI Recruitment 2026. Check zone-wise and category-wise vacancy details in the tables below.
