RBI Junior Engineer (Civil/ Electrical) Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria

Educational Qualification (Mandatory): Junior Engineer (Civil): Minimum three years Diploma in Civil Engineering OR Degree in Civil Engineering with prescribed minimum percentage of marks. Junior Engineer (Electrical): Minimum three years Diploma in Electrical/Electrical & Electronic Engineering OR Degree in Electrical/Electrical & Electronic Engineering with prescribed minimum percentage of marks (relaxation for SC/ST/PwBD as per rules).

Experience (Mandatory): For Diploma holders: At least 2 years' post-qualification experience. For Degree holders: At least 1-year post-qualification experience (or 1-year graduate apprenticeship training in a PSU) in the relevant field as detailed in the notification.

Nationality: The candidate must be either a citizen of India, or a subject of Nepal/Bhutan, or a Tibetan refugee who came over to India before 1st January 1962, or a person of Indian origin who has migrated from specified countries with the intention of permanently settling in India (with eligibility certificate where required).

RBI Junior Engineer (Civil/ Electrical) Recruitment 2026 Age Limit

The age limit to apply for RBI Junior Engineer (Civil/Electrical) Recruitment 2026 is 20 to 35 years. The cutoff date for determining the age limit is 01 April 2026. Age relaxation will be provided to reserved category candidates as per government rules.

Category Minimum Age Maximum Age
General / Unreserved 20 years 30 years
SC / ST 20 years 35 years (5 years relaxation)
OBC 20 years 33 years (3 years relaxation)
Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) 20 years 40 years (GEN/EWS) / 43 years (OBC) / 45 years (SC/ST)
Ex-Servicemen To the extent of service rendered in Armed Forces plus additional 3 years (subject to maximum 50 years)
Widows / Divorced women / Women judicially separated (not re-married) 20 years 35 years (40 years for SC/ST)

Note: Age is calculated as on 01 April 2026. Cumulative age relaxation will not be available. Candidates born not earlier than 02/04/1996 and not later than 01/04/2006 (both days inclusive) are eligible.

RBI Junior Engineer Recruitment 2026 Salary Structure

Selected candidates will draw a starting basic pay of ₹47,700/- per month (i.e., ₹29,000/- plus nine advance increments admissible to Junior Engineers) in the scale of ₹29,000 - 1,700(3) - 34,100 - 2,040(4) - 42,260 - 2,720(6) - 58,580 - 2,950(2) - 64,480 - 3,370(3) - 74,590 - 4,050(1) - 78,640 and other allowances, as admissible from time to time. At present, initial monthly Gross emoluments for Junior Engineer (Civil/ Electrical) is approximately ₹91,309/-.

Particulars Details
Basic Pay (Starting) ₹47,700/- p.m.
Pay Scale ₹29,000 - 78,640 (with increments as per scale)
Gross Emoluments (approx.) ₹91,309/- p.m. (including allowances)
House Rent Allowance 15% of Pay (if accommodation not provided by the Bank)
Other Benefits Bank's accommodation (subject to availability), allowances for knowledge updation, briefcase, furnishing, dispensary, medical reimbursement, festival advance, LFC, concessional loans, Gratuity, New Pension Scheme etc.

RBI Junior Engineer (Civil/ Electrical) Recruitment 2026 Online Application

The RBI Junior Engineer (Civil/Electrical) Application Form 2026 is available in online mode only on the official website of the Reserve Bank of India (www.rbi.org.in). Eligible candidates must complete and submit the application form before the last date 06 May 2026 (11:59 PM) to avoid last-minute issues.

The application process includes registration, filling in personal and educational details, uploading documents, and payment of the application fee.

RBI Junior Engineer (Civil/ Electrical) Recruitment 2026 Online Apply Link

RBI Junior Engineer (Civil/ Electrical) Recruitment 2026 Selection Process

The selection for RBI Junior Engineer Recruitment 2026 will be based on the following:

Online Examination (300 marks): Objective test consisting of English Language, Engineering Discipline (Paper I & II), General Intelligence and Reasoning. The examination is tentatively scheduled for 06 June 2026.

Language Proficiency Test (LPT): Candidates provisionally selected from the online examination will have to undergo a Language Proficiency Test in the Official/Local Language/s of the zone concerned. Candidate not proficient in the Official/Local Language/s would be disqualified.

Document Verification & Medical Examination: Final selection will be on the basis of performance in the online examination (in order of merit), qualifying in LPT, medical fitness and verification of certificates to the Bank's satisfaction.

RBI Junior Engineer (Civil/ Electrical) Recruitment 2026 Documents Required

Candidates must upload the following at the time of online application:

Recent passport size photograph, Signature, Left Thumb Impression, Hand-written declaration (in English) as per specifications given in the notification.

Note: At the time of LPT / Document Verification, candidates will be required to produce original documents related to age, educational qualification, experience, category, PwBD, Ex-Servicemen etc. as applicable.

Photo & Signature: Scanned as per exact specifications (Photograph: 20-50 KB, Signature: 10-20 KB, Left Thumb Impression: 20-50 KB, Hand-written Declaration: 50-100 KB).

RBI Junior Engineer (Civil/ Electrical) Recruitment 2026 Application Fee

Sl. No. Category Fee
01 SC / ST / PwBD / EXS ₹50/- + 18% GST (Intimation Charges only)
02 OBC / General / EWS ₹450/- + 18% GST (Examination Fee + Intimation Charges)

Fee can be paid online via Debit Card / Credit Card / Internet Banking / IMPS / Cash Cards / Mobile Wallets. Staff candidates are exempted from payment of fee/intimation charges. Bank transaction charges will have to be borne by the candidate. Fee once paid will not be refunded.

How to Apply for RBI Junior Engineer (Civil/ Electrical) Recruitment 2026

Eligible candidates for RBI Junior Engineer Recruitment 2026 must submit their application online through the official website www.rbi.org.in within the specified last date. The online application process is as follows:

Step 1 - Registration: Visit the Bank's website www.rbi.org.in and click on the link "Recruitment for the Post of Junior Engineer (Civil/ Electrical) - Panel Year 2026". Choose "Click here for New Registration" and enter basic details. A Provisional Registration Number and Password will be generated. Upload scanned photograph, signature, left thumb impression and handwritten declaration as per specifications.

Step 2 - Application Submission: Login with Provisional Registration Number and Password. Fill all required details in the online application form. Validate the details and save the application using "SAVE AND NEXT" facility. Upload required images and preview the entire form before final submission.

Step 3 - Final Submission & Fee Payment: After verifying all entries, click on "COMPLETE REGISTRATION". Proceed to payment of applicable fee/intimation charges online. On successful payment, an e-Receipt will be generated. Download and print the final submitted application form for future reference.

Edit Window: After the closing date, a short Edit Window (dates to be notified) will be available for limited corrections (Exam Centre, Father's Name, Mother's Name) on payment of ₹200/- correction fee.

Note: Candidates can apply only for one post and one zone. Offline applications will not be accepted. Incomplete applications or applications without proper photograph/signature will be rejected. Candidates are advised to apply well before the last date to avoid last-minute technical issues.

RBI Junior Engineer (Civil/ Electrical) Recruitment 2026 - Important Instructions

  • Candidates must possess all required educational qualifications, experience and certificates by the closing date of online application (06 May 2026). Certificates issued after the closing date will not be accepted.
  • Reservation benefits are available only as per prevailing Government guidelines. Candidates must upload correct category certificate in the prescribed format. PwBD candidates must possess a permanent disability certificate issued as per RPwD Act 2016.
  • Use of mobile phones or any electronic devices is strictly prohibited in the examination premises. Candidates spreading misleading information or using unfair means will face disqualification and legal action.
  • The Bank's decision regarding eligibility, qualification and candidature of candidates will be final. All information related to the selection will be published on the Bank's website www.rbi.org.in only; candidates must regularly check the website.
  • Candidates can contact the help facility through the link http://cgrs.ibps.in/ for any technical issues (mention the exact recruitment name in the subject).

UP Cooperative Bank (UPCISB) Selection Process 2026

  • The selection process consists of an online Preliminary Examination (if required) and a Main Examination conducted by UPCISB.
  • Based on the Preliminary Exam, up to 15 times the number of vacancies will be shortlisted for the Main Exam.
  • On the basis of Main Exam result, up to 3 times the number of posts will be called for interview (where applicable) and up to 5 times the number of posts for proficiency test, wherever prescribed.
  • Both Preliminary and Main examinations will be objective-type online tests with multiple choice questions and negative marking of 1/4 mark for each wrong answer.

How to Apply for UP Cooperative Bank (UPCISB) Recruitment 2026

  • Candidates must apply online only through the official website of U.P. Co-operative Institutional Service Board: https://www.upcisb.upsdc.gov.in.
  • First, complete “New Registration” by entering name, contact details and email ID to generate a registration number and password.
  • Fill in the online application form carefully using the “Save and Next” option, upload scanned photograph, signature, left-hand thumb impression and handwritten declaration as per the prescribed specifications.
  • Pay the application fee through the integrated payment gateway, ensure e-Receipt generation, and take a printout of the final submitted application form and fee receipt for future reference.

UP Cooperative Bank (UPCISB) Salary 2026

  • The notification provides detailed pay scales for each post, such as specific minimum and maximum pay in the respective pay bands for Manager, Junior Manager, Assistant/Cashier, Assistant Manager, Accountant, Junior Engineer, Assistant Engineer and other posts.

  • Pay scales differ institution-wise and post-wise (for example, different pay bands for Junior Manager in different District Co-operative Banks), and all posts are permanent with regular pay as per the respective co-operative institution’s rules.

Important Instructions for Candidates

  • Candidates must thoroughly read and understand all instructions in the advertisement before filling the online application form.
  • The photograph, signature, thumb impression and handwritten declaration must be clear and as per the prescribed size and format, otherwise the application may be rejected.
  • No change is permitted in key details after clicking on “Complete Registration”, so all information (name, father’s name, etc.) must match official certificates.
  • Candidates should keep their registered email ID and mobile number active till completion of the recruitment process for receiving admit card and other communication from UPCISB.
  • Minimum efficiency standard (qualifying marks) and negative marking rules in the exam must be noted carefully, as incorrect answers will attract penalty.

Punjab and Sind Bank SO Vacancy 2026 Notification OUT

A recent official notification has been released by Punjab & Sind Bank for the recruitment of Specialist Officers (Security) in MMGS-II on a regular basis. As per the notification, a total of 27 Security Manager vacancies have been announced across various categories, offering a valuable opportunity for candidates with defense or police background to secure a stable banking career.

The online application process will begin from 15 April 2026 and will continue until 05 May 2026, giving eligible candidates a limited window to apply through the official website. This recruitment is particularly important as such lateral entry opportunities in public sector banks are not released frequently. Candidates are advised to carefully review the eligibility criteria, including experience requirements as a Commissioned Officer or Police/Paramilitary Officer, before applying.

Punjab & Sind Bank, a Government of India undertaking, has clearly stated that the number of vacancies is provisional and may change based on requirements. The detailed selection process, including written test, screening, and interview, will be conducted as per bank norms, and further updates will be published only on the official website. Candidates should stay alert for updates and ensure timely application submission to avoid last-minute issues.

Indian Bank Recruitment 2026

Recruitment of Specialist Officer in Indian Bank in 2026: The Indian Bank has released the Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 Notification for 350 vacancies across various functional areas and scales (Scale I, II, III) in branches and offices of Indian Bank across India. Eligible candidates holding qualifications such as B.Tech/B.E, CA, MBA/PGDM, MCA and other prescribed degrees with the required experience can apply online for these Specialist positions. The online application process started on April 8, 2026 and will remain open until April 28, 2026. Interested applicants can submit their applications through the official website, indianbank.bank.in, via the “Recruitment of Specialist Officers – 2026” link or directly through the IBPS registration portal at ibpsreg.ibps.in/ibsofeb26/.

Indian Bank Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF Download

The Indian Bank Specialist Officer Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF has been released on 08-04-2026 at indianbank.bank.in. Selection Process

Selection will be made through shortlisting of applications followed by Interview (or Online Examination + Interview, if conducted by the Bank).

  • Candidates fulfilling the eligibility criteria will be shortlisted for Interview (or Online Test + Interview) based on the information furnished in the online application and supporting documents.
  • The Bank reserves the right to conduct Online Examination / Group Discussion / Interview or any combination thereof as per its requirement.
  • Final selection will be based on the candidate’s performance in the selection process, subject to verification of documents and medical fitness.
  • The Bank may shortlist candidates in the ratio decided by the Bank and call them for Interview.
  • Merit list will be prepared post-Interview and provisional offer of appointment will be issued to the selected candidates.
  • The decision of the Bank in all matters relating to selection shall be final and binding.

How to Apply

Application Mode: Online only through the official website of Indian Bank. No other mode of application will be accepted.

Steps to Apply:

  1. Visit the official website of Indian Bank and go to the “Recruitment” / “Careers” section.
  2. Click on the link for “Recruitment of Specialist Officers - 2026” and register using a valid email ID and mobile number.
  3. Fill the online application form carefully with all mandatory details (personal information, educational qualification, experience, category etc.).
  4. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature and all required documents/certificates (educational, experience, category, PwBD etc.) as per specifications.
  5. Pay the requisite application fee / intimation charges online.
  6. Submit the application and take a printout of the final submitted application form for future reference.
  7. A candidate can apply for only one post. In case of multiple applications, only the latest valid (completed) application will be retained.

General Instructions

  • Cut-off date for determining eligibility (age, qualification, experience, etc.) is 01-04-2026.
  • Only one application should be submitted by any candidate. Multiple applications will lead to cancellation of candidature.
  • Candidates must ensure that they fulfil all the eligibility criteria mentioned in the advertisement. Mere submission of application does not guarantee eligibility.
  • The number of vacancies is provisional and may vary according to the actual requirement of the Bank.
  • Reservation for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD will be provided as per Government of India guidelines. PwBD reservation is on horizontal basis.
  • Candidates belonging to reserved categories must upload valid category certificates issued on or before the last date of registration.
  • All educational qualifications and experience must be from Government recognized Universities / Institutions and should be active as on the date of application.
  • The Bank reserves the right to cancel / modify the recruitment process at any stage without assigning any reason.
  • Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification of the candidature.
  • Decision of the Bank in all matters relating to eligibility, acceptance or rejection of applications, mode of selection, etc. shall be final and binding on the candidates.
  • All future communications (call letters, results, etc.) will be sent only through the registered email ID and SMS. Candidates are advised to keep the same email ID and mobile number active till completion of the recruitment process.
  • Any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be subject to the jurisdiction of Courts in Chennai only

Thousands of candidates who appeared in the mains examination held on 21st November 2025 can now check their detailed scores online.

How to Check SBI Clerk Mains Score Card 2026?

Candidates can download their SBI Clerk Mains Score Card 2026 by following this simple process:

  1. Visit the official website: sbi.co.in or bank.sbi/careers.
  2. Go to the "Careers" section and find the link for "Recruitment of Junior Associates (CRPD/CR/2025-26/06)" or "SBI Clerk Mains Score Card 2026".
  3. Click on the scorecard download link.
  4. Enter your Registration Number/Roll Number and Password/Date of Birth.
  5. Submit the details to view your section-wise marks, total score, and other details.
  6. Download and save the scorecard PDF for future reference.

SBI Clerk Mains Merit List 2026 - What's Inside?

The SBI Clerk Mains merit list 2026 (released on 27th February 2026) contains roll numbers of provisionally selected candidates. SBI prepares state-wise and category-wise lists for final selection.

Merit List Contains:

  • Roll Numbers of qualified candidates
  • State-wise selection
  • Category-wise allocation (Gen/OBC/SC/ST/EWS/PwBD)
  • Provisional selection status

Types of Lists Released:

  1. Provisional Select List: Main merit list of qualified candidates
  2. Category-Wise Lists: Separate for different categories and states
  3. Waiting List: For future vacancies if selected candidates do not join

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The latest South Indian Bank Limited (SIB) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for ITGC Auditor, RED Team Lead, and VAPT posts! Published on 2nd March 2026 at southindianbank.bank.in, this new recruitment drive offers multiple vacancies for eligible candidates. Applicants within the maximum age limit of 35 years and possessing a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science / Information Technology or B.Tech in EC / CS / IT can apply online from 2nd March 2026 to 23rd April 2026. Find all details about the SIB ITGC Auditor, RED Team Lead, and VAPT new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

South Indian Bank ITGC Auditor, RED Team Lead and VAPT Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

South Indian Bank has issued the official recruitment notification for specialist posts including Vulnerability Assessment & Penetration Testing (VAPT), IT General Controls (ITGC) Audit, and RED Team Lead. The notification contains complete information about eligibility, age limit, experience, scale of appointment, salary, selection process, probation, posting location, document upload instructions, and online application process. Candidates should read the complete notification carefully before submitting the application form.

  • Download South Indian Bank ITGC Auditor, RED Team Lead and VAPT Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

South Indian Bank ITGC Auditor, RED Team Lead and VAPT Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organisation Name South Indian Bank Limited (SIB)
Post Name ITGC Auditor, RED Team Lead, VAPT
Department IS Audit Function
Total Vacancies Not Specified
Scale Scale I / II / III (depending on post and experience)
Application Mode Online only
Application Start Date 02-03-2026
Last Date to Apply 23-04-2026 (extended from 09/03/2026)
Eligibility Cut-Off Date 31-01-2026
Age Limit Maximum 35 years
Salary IBA Scale I/II/III + DA, HRA, Special Allowance & Performance Linked Incentive
Official Website www.southindianbank.bank.in

South Indian Bank ITGC Auditor, RED Team Lead and VAPT Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Eligibility Cut-Off Date 31-01-2026
Online Application Start Date 02-03-2026
Last Date for Online Application 23-04-2026

South Indian Bank ITGC Auditor, RED Team Lead and VAPT Recruitment 2026 Vacancy Details

South Indian Bank has invited applications for multiple specialist posts under the IS Audit Function. The notification does not mention the exact number of vacancies. Candidates can apply for the suitable role based on their qualification, experience, and professional certifications.

Post Name Scale
Vulnerability Assessment & Penetration Testing (VAPT) Scale II / III
IT General Controls (ITGC) Audit Scale I / II
RED Team Lead Scale I / II

South Indian Bank ITGC Auditor, RED Team Lead and VAPT Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria

Post Name Educational Qualification Experience Preferred / Desirable Certifications
VAPT Bachelor's degree in Computer Science / Information Technology / B.Tech in EC / CS / IT with 50% marks Minimum 2 years of experience in conducting IT/IS audit or application security testing OSCP, OSWE, eWPTX, CEH, eJPT; basic scripting knowledge in Python, Bash or similar is desirable
ITGC Auditor Bachelor's degree in Computer Science / Information Technology / B.Tech in EC / CS / IT with 50% marks Minimum 2 years of experience in conducting IT/IS audit, preferably in a bank or financial institution CISA, ISO 27001 Lead Auditor
RED Team Lead Bachelor's degree in Computer Science / Information Technology / B.Tech in EC / CS / IT with 50% marks Minimum 2 years of experience in conducting RED team activities CRTP, CRTO, OSCP

South Indian Bank ITGC Auditor, RED Team Lead and VAPT Recruitment 2026 Age Limit

The maximum age limit is 35 years as on 31-01-2026. Candidates must be born on or after 01-02-1991. SC/ST candidates are eligible for age relaxation of 5 years as per the notification conditions.

Category Maximum Age Age Relaxation
General / Other Categories 35 Years Nil
SC / ST 40 Years 5 Years

South Indian Bank ITGC Auditor, RED Team Lead and VAPT Recruitment 2026 Application Fee

South Indian Bank has not prescribed any application fee for this recruitment. Candidates can complete the online registration process without payment of any fee.

Category Application Fee
All Candidates Nil

South Indian Bank ITGC Auditor, RED Team Lead and VAPT Recruitment 2026 Pay Scale & Benefits

Selected candidates will be appointed in IBA-approved Scale I, II, or III depending on the post, qualification, and experience. In addition to basic pay, candidates will be eligible for Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), Special Allowance, and Performance Linked Incentive as applicable under the bank’s rules.

Pay Details
IBA Approved Scale I / II / III + DA + HRA + Special Allowance + Other Allowances + Performance Linked Incentive

South Indian Bank ITGC Auditor, RED Team Lead and VAPT Recruitment 2026 Selection Process

The selection process consists of shortlisting of applications followed by preliminary and/or personal interview for shortlisted candidates. Mere fulfilment of the eligibility criteria will not guarantee selection or interview call. The bank reserves the right to modify the selection process at any stage.

Stage Details
Stage 1: Shortlisting Applications will be screened based on qualification, experience, certifications, and suitability as per bank requirements.
Stage 2: Preliminary and/or Personal Interview Only shortlisted candidates will be invited for the recruitment process.
Final Selection Based on performance in the interview and other criteria decided by the bank.

General Information / Important Instructions for Candidates

Candidates must apply only through the official online portal of South Indian Bank. Applications submitted through any other mode will not be accepted. The recruitment is subject to medical fitness, background verification, police clearance, and credit/CIBIL check as per bank rules.

  • Scale of appointment will be fixed by the bank based on qualification and experience.
  • Probation period is 1 year from the date of joining.
  • Posting location is Ernakulam initially, but candidates must be willing to serve anywhere in India.
  • Call letters and future communication will be sent only through registered email ID.
  • Multiple applications for the same role will lead to disqualification.
  • Canvassing in any form will be treated as disqualification.
  • All corrigendum, updates, and notices will be published only on the official bank website.
  • Original documents will be verified at a later stage, and candidature remains provisional until verification.
  • Failure to produce valid supporting documents will lead to rejection of candidature.

How to Apply for South Indian Bank ITGC Auditor, RED Team Lead and VAPT Recruitment 2026

The application process is completely online. Candidates must visit the official website of South Indian Bank and apply through the Careers section within the prescribed dates. Before starting the registration, candidates should keep scanned copies of photograph, signature, resume, and a valid email ID ready.

  • Visit the official website: www.southindianbank.bank.in
  • Go to About Us > Careers section.
  • Open the recruitment link for IS Audit Function posts.
  • Click on the Apply Online link.
  • Register with a valid personal email ID.
  • Fill in the online application form carefully with correct details.
  • Upload photograph (378x437 pixels, max 50 KB, white background, colour passport size).
  • Upload signature (140x110 pixels, max 50 KB, black ink on white paper).
  • Upload resume in PDF format (maximum 1 MB).
  • Verify all details before final submission, as changes may not be allowed later.
  • Submit the application form and note the system-generated User ID / Application Reference ID.
  • Check the registered email for application confirmation and future updates.
  • No physical copy of the application form needs to be sent to the bank.

Documents Required for South Indian Bank ITGC Auditor, RED Team Lead and VAPT Recruitment 2026

S.No. Document
1 Recent passport-size photograph
2 Scanned signature
3 Updated resume in PDF format
4 Proof of date of birth
5 Educational qualification certificates and mark sheets
6 Work experience certificates
7 Professional certification documents, if applicable
8 Valid caste certificate for SC/ST candidates, if applicable
9 Any other supporting documents required by the bank

Reserve Bank of India has released the RBI Medical Consultant Recruitment 2026 notification for 2 Medical Consultant posts on a contract basis. This opportunity is ideal for qualified medical professionals seeking part-time government jobs in the banking sector. Candidates with an MBBS degree and at least two years of post-qualification experience in the Allopathic system of medicine are eligible to apply for this recruitment.

Selected candidates will be paid a remuneration of ₹1,000 per hour along with additional benefits such as conveyance allowance and mobile reimbursement. The selection process will be based on an interview followed by medical examination and document verification. Interested and eligible candidates must submit their offline application form before the last date, 05 May 2026, to be considered for this opportunity.

Last Date 05 May 2026

    • Minimum of two (2) years post qualification experience practicing Allopathic system of medicine in any hospital or clinic as a Medical Practitioner.

RBI Medical Consultant Salary 2026

  • Remuneration: ₹1,000/- per hour
  • Conveyance expenses: ₹1,000/- per month (deducted from monthly remuneration)
  • Mobile charges reimbursement: ₹1000/- per month

RRB NTPC Undergraduate Exam Date 2026 (Released) - Check Schedule & Details

RRB NTPC Undergraduate Exam Date 2026: The Railway Recruitment Board (RRB) has officially released the exam date for the NTPC Undergraduate recruitment under CEN 07/2025. Candidates who have applied for the Undergraduate level posts can check the complete exam schedule below. The CBT-1 examination is scheduled to be held from 7th May to 21st June 2026 (specifically on 7th, 8th, 9th May and 13th, 14th, 16th to 21st June 2026). All eligible candidates are advised to download their admit cards and prepare accordingly.

Latest Update: RRB has announced the exam date for NTPC Undergraduate positions. The CBT-1 examination will be conducted from 7th May to 21st June 2026 at various centers across India. Candidates can download the exam date notification from the official RRB websites.

Key Highlights - RRB NTPC Undergraduate Exam Date 2026

  • Organization: Railway Recruitment Board (RRB)
  • Post Name: NTPC Undergraduate (Various Posts like Junior Clerk cum Typist, Accounts Clerk cum Typist, Trains Clerk, Commercial cum Ticket Clerk)
  • Exam Date: 7th, 8th, 9th May 2026 and 13th, 14th, 16th to 21st June 2026 (CBT-1)

RRB NTPC Undergraduate Exam Pattern 2026

Understanding the exam pattern is crucial for effective preparation. Here's what candidates need to know about the RRB NTPC Undergraduate examination:

  • Exam Mode: Online Computer Based Test (CBT)
  • Total Marks: 100
  • Number of Questions: 100
  • Duration: 90 Minutes
  • Negative Marking: Yes (1/3rd mark deducted for each wrong answer)
  • Subjects/Sections: General Awareness (40 Qs/40 Marks), Mathematics (30 Qs/30 Marks), General Intelligence and Reasoning (30 Qs/30 Marks)

Preparation Tips for RRB NTPC Undergraduate Exam

With the exam date announced, candidates should focus on smart preparation strategies:

1. Create a Study Schedule: Divide your time wisely among all subjects based on your strengths and weaknesses.

2. Focus on Previous Year Papers: Solve at least 5-10 previous year question papers to understand the pattern and difficulty level.

3. Take Mock Tests: Regular mock tests will help you manage time and identify areas needing improvement.

4. Revise Regularly: Make short notes and revise them daily, especially for GK and current affairs.

5. Stay Updated: Keep yourself updated with current affairs, especially related to Indian Railways and general awareness.

RRB NTPC Undergraduate Admit Card 2026 - When to Download?

The RRB will release the admit card for NTPC Undergraduate examination approximately 4 days before the exam date. Candidates can download their hall tickets from the official website using their registration number and password. City Intimation Slip will be available 10 days before the exam.

Documents Required on Exam Day:

  • Printed copy of Admit Card
  • Valid Photo ID Proof (Aadhar Card/PAN Card/Driving License/Voter ID)
  • Recent passport-size photographs
  • Required stationery items (as per instructions)
  • Original Aadhaar or e-Aadhaar (biometric verification mandatory)

What After the RRB NTPC Undergraduate Exam?

After successfully appearing in the RRB NTPC Undergraduate examination, candidates should:

1. Result Declaration: The result is typically declared after the completion of all stages. Results will be available on the official website.

2. Answer Key: Preliminary answer keys may be released after the exam. Candidates can raise objections if any discrepancy is found.

3. Next Stage: Qualified candidates will be called for CBT-2, Typing Skill Test (for applicable posts), Document Verification, and Medical Examination.

Disclaimer: The information provided above is based on the official notification released by Railway Recruitment Board. Candidates are advised to visit the official website www.rrbcdg.gov.in for the most accurate and updated information.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

RRB Group D Eligibility Criteria 2026 state that candidates must be Indian nationals (or as per specified rules) who have passed Class 10/ITI/NAC, are within the prescribed 18–33 years age limit (with relaxations for reserved categories), and meet the required medical and physical standards as detailed in the official notification.

The RRB Group D Notification 2026 (CEN 09/2025) defines these criteria for Level-1 posts such as Track Maintainer, Pointsman, Assistant Pointsman, Assistant Loco Shed, and others, with a total of around 22,195 vacancies (revised figures noted in some sources). The recruitment process includes a Computer-Based Test (CBT), Physical Efficiency Test (PET), Document Verification, and Medical Examination.

RRB Group D Eligibility Criteria 2026 – Quick Overview

Here are the key points at a glance:

  • Minimum Education: 10th pass (Matriculation) from a recognized board, or ITI from NCVT/SCVT, or National Apprenticeship Certificate (NAC) in relevant trade.

  • Age Range: Generally 18–33 years (calculated as on the cutoff date mentioned in the notification, such as 01.01.2026).

  • Nationality Requirement: Primarily Indian citizens; certain other categories like subjects of Nepal/Bhutan or specified migrants are eligible with proper certificates.

  • Physical Efficiency Test (PET) and Medical Standards: Mandatory qualifying stages after CBT.

  • Category-wise Age Relaxation: Available for OBC, SC/ST, Ex-servicemen, PwBD, and others as per rules.

Parameter

Details (as per 2026 notification)

RRB Group D Level 1 Posts 2026

Railway Recruitment Board (RRB)

Age Limit (General)

CEN 09/2025 (RRB Group D Notification 2026)

Check the RRB Group D Notification 2026 PDF and Apply Online link below before applying (links available on official RRB websites like rrbapply.gov.in or regional portals during active periods).

RRB Group D Educational Qualification 2026

The minimum educational qualification for RRB Group D 2026 requires candidates to have:

  • Passed Class 10/Matriculation from a recognized board, or

  • ITI certificate from NCVT/SCVT in a relevant trade, or

  • National Apprenticeship Certificate (NAC) granted by NCVT.

Equivalence like NIOS/open schooling is generally accepted if recognized and mentioned in the official notification—always verify the exact clause in the CEN PDF. Final-year or appearing candidates are typically not eligible; the qualification must be completed by the application closing date.

Common doubts:

  • Gap years or backlogs do not disqualify if the certificate is obtained.

  • Subject stream usually does not matter for non-technical posts.

  • Always cross-check with the official notification to avoid issues.

RRB Group D Age Limit 2026

The general age limit for RRB Group D 2026 is 18 to 33 years, calculated as on the date specified in the notification (e.g., 01.01.2026). Candidates must fall within this range before applying.

Category

Minimum Age

Maximum Age (Base)

UR / General

RRB Group D Age Relaxation 2026 (Category-wise)

RRB Group D age relaxation 2026 is provided over the upper age limit as follows (exact details in official CEN):

Category

Age Relaxation over Upper Age Limit

OBC (Non-Creamy Layer)

3 years

SC / ST

5 years

Ex-servicemen

As per length of service (generally 3–8 years or more)

PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities)

10 years (additional for OBC/SC/ST)

Railway staff / Quasi-admin staff

As per norms and service length

Age relaxation is not automatic valid category certificates are mandatory at document verification. Confirm exact figures in the official CEN for 2026.

RRB Group D Nationality Criteria 2026

Under RRB Group D nationality criteria, candidates must be:

  • A citizen of India, or

  • A subject of Nepal or Bhutan, or

  • A Tibetan refugee who came to India before January 1, 1962 (with intent to settle permanently), or

  • A person of Indian origin migrated from specified countries (Pakistan, Burma, etc.) with an eligibility certificate from the Government of India.

Most applicants qualify as Indian citizens.

RRB Group D Physical Eligibility & PET 2026

The RRB Group D physical standards include two stages: PET (qualifying) and medical examination.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET) Requirements

PET is qualifying (pass/fail) and conducted for shortlisted CBT candidates. Typical requirements (as per notification; may vary slightly):

  • Male candidates: Running a specified distance in given time, lifting/carrying weight over distance.

  • Female candidates: Adjusted distance/weight parameters.

Only those passing PET proceed further.

RRB Group D Medical Standards 2026

The medical exam follows document verification and includes vision (distant/near), color vision, blood pressure, cardiac, hearing, and general fitness checks. Posts fall under A-2, B-1, C-1, etc., categories candidates must meet the standards for allotted posts.

Common disqualifying reasons include poor eyesight beyond limits, uncontrolled chronic conditions, or major disabilities incompatible with duties. Exact conditions are in the official rules.

Important Documents Required for RRB Group D Eligibility Verification

Candidates need these for various stages (especially document verification):

  • 10th/Matriculation certificate and mark sheet.

  • ITI/NAC certificate (if applicable).

  • Category certificates (SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS in central format).

  • PwBD certificate (if claiming).

  • Ex-servicemen discharge certificate.

  • Photo ID (Aadhaar, Voter ID, etc.).

  • Passport-size photographs (per specs).

Who Is Not Eligible for RRB Group D 2026?

  • Candidates below 18 or above 33 years (even after relaxation).

  • Those without required 10th/ITI/NAC by cutoff date.

  • Non-eligible nationality or missing certificates.

  • Failing PET or medical exam.

  • Incomplete, incorrect, or forged documents.

  • Debarred by RRB or with disqualifying background (if specified).

This bumper railway vacancy is a major opportunity for candidates preparing for latest Railway Govt Jobs as well as state-wise railway jobs, because applications for RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 can be submitted online for any participating RRB including Ahmedabad, Chennai, Kolkata, Mumbai, Secunderabad and others.

Eligible candidates aged 18 to 30 years as on 01 July 2026 can apply for RRB Assistant Loco Pilot posts through online mode only from 15 May 2026 to 14 June 2026 (23:59 hrs) by visiting the official recruitment portal rrbapply.gov.in and the respective RRB websites mentioned in CEN 01/2026.

How to Raise Objection Against RRB Technician Answer Key 2026 ?

If you disagree with any answer in the provisional key, follow these steps:
